contentBlocks.original: अस्य श्री महालक्ष्मीहृदयस्तोत्र महामन्त्रस्य भार्गव ऋषिः, अनुष्टुपादीनि नानाछन्दांसि, आद्यादि श्रीमहालक्ष्मीर्देवता, श्रीं बीजं, ह्रीं शक्तिः, ऐं कीलकं, आद्यादिमहालक्ष्मी प्रसादसिद्ध्यर्थं जपे विनियोगः ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: asya śrī mahālakṣmīhṛdayastōtra mahāmantrasya bhārgava ṛṣiḥ, anuṣṭupādīni nānāChandāṃsi, ādyādi śrīmahālakṣmīrdēvatā, śrīṃ bījaṃ, hrīṃ śaktiḥ, aiṃ kīlakaṃ, ādyādimahālakṣmī prasādasiddhyarthaṃ japē viniyōgaḥ ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Viniyoga (ritual dedication): Of this Śrī Mahālakṣmī Hṛdaya Stotram Mahāmantra — the seer (ṛṣi) is Bhārgava (Śukrāchārya); the metres (chandas) are Anuṣṭup and various others; the deity (dēvatā) is Ādi Śrī Mahālakṣmī; the seed-syllable (bīja) is Śrīṃ; the power-syllable (śakti) is Hrīṃ; the pin-syllable (kīlaka) is Aiṃ. The purpose (viniyoga) is the attainment of the four goals of life (dharma, artha, kāma, mōkṣa) and all good fortune.

contentBlocks.original:
ऋष्यादिन्यासः
ॐ भार्गवृषये नमः शिरसि ।
ॐ अनुष्टुपादिनानाछन्दोभ्यो नमो मुखे ।
ॐ आद्यादिश्रीमहालक्ष्मी देवतायै नमो हृदये ।
ॐ श्रीं बीजाय नमो गुह्ये ।
ॐ ह्रीं शक्तये नमः पादयोः ।
ॐ ऐं कीलकाय नमो नाभौ ।
ॐ विनियोगाय नमः सर्वाङ्गे ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ṛṣyādinyāsaḥ
ōṃ bhārgavṛṣayē namaḥ śirasi ।
ōṃ anuṣṭupādinānāChandōbhyō namō mukhē ।
ōṃ ādyādiśrīmahālakṣmī dēvatāyai namō hṛdayē ।
ōṃ śrīṃ bījāya namō guhyē ।
ōṃ hrīṃ śaktayē namaḥ pādayōḥ ।
ōṃ aiṃ kīlakāya namō nābhau ।
ōṃ viniyōgāya namaḥ sarvāṅgē ।
contentBlocks.translation: Ṛṣyādi-nyāsa (ritual assignment of the mantra components to parts of the body): Oṃ — salutation to the Bhārgava seer upon the head. Oṃ — salutation to the Anuṣṭup and various metres upon the mouth. Oṃ — salutation to Ādi Śrī Mahālakṣmī, the deity, upon the heart. Oṃ Śrīṃ — salutation to the seed-syllable upon the navel. Oṃ Hrīṃ — salutation to the power-syllable upon the secret centre. Oṃ Aiṃ — salutation to the pin-syllable upon the feet. Oṃ — salutation to the entirety of the mantra in all the limbs.

contentBlocks.original:
करन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं अङ्गुष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं तर्जनीभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ऐं मध्यमाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ श्रीं अनामिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं कनिष्ठिकाभ्यां नमः ।
ॐ ऐं करतल करपृष्ठाभ्यां नमः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
karanyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ aṅguṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ tarjanībhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ aiṃ madhyamābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ anāmikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ kaniṣṭhikābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
ōṃ aiṃ karatala karapṛṣṭhābhyāṃ namaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Kara-nyāsa (assignment of bīja-syllables to the fingers and palms): Oṃ Śrīṃ — salutation to the thumbs. Oṃ Hrīṃ — salutation to the index fingers. Oṃ Aiṃ — salutation to the middle fingers. Oṃ Śrīṃ — salutation to the ring fingers. Oṃ Hrīṃ — salutation to the little fingers. Oṃ Aiṃ — salutation to the back and palm of both hands together.

contentBlocks.original:
अङ्गन्यासः
ॐ श्रीं हृदयाय नमः ।
ॐ ह्रीं शिरसे स्वाहा ।
ॐ ऐं शिखायै वषट् ।
ॐ श्रीं कवचाय हुम् ।
ॐ ह्रीं नेत्रत्रयाय वौषट् ।
ॐ ऐं अस्त्राय फट् ।
ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं ऐं इति दिग्बन्धः ।
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṅganyāsaḥ
ōṃ śrīṃ hṛdayāya namaḥ ।
ōṃ hrīṃ śirasē svāhā ।
ōṃ aiṃ śikhāyai vaṣaṭ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ kavachāya hum ।
ōṃ hrīṃ nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ ।
ōṃ aiṃ astrāya phaṭ ।
ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ aiṃ iti digbandhaḥ ।
contentBlocks.translation: Aṅga-nyāsa (assignment of bīja-syllables to the six main body parts): Oṃ Śrīṃ — salutation (Hṛdayāya namaḥ) to the heart. Oṃ Hrīṃ — offering (Śirasē svāhā) to the head. Oṃ Aiṃ — oblation (Śikhāyai vaṣaṭ) to the topknot. Oṃ Śrīṃ — protection (Kavachāya hum) as armour. Oṃ Hrīṃ — triple-eye oblation (Nētratrayāya vauṣaṭ). Oṃ Aiṃ — weapon-consecration (Astrāya phaṭ) to the divine weapon.

contentBlocks.original:
अथ ध्यानम्
हस्तद्वयेन कमले धारयन्तीं स्वलीलया ।
हारनूपुरसंयुक्तां लक्ष्मीं देवीं विचिन्तये ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atha dhyānam
hastadvayēna kamalē dhārayantīṃ svalīlayā ।
hāranūpurasaṃyuktāṃ lakṣmīṃ dēvīṃ vichintayē ॥
contentBlocks.translation: First meditation verse (Dhyāna 1): I contemplate Goddess Lakṣmī who effortlessly holds two lotuses in her two hands, adorned with garlands and anklet-bells, the eternal divine Lakṣmī.
contentBlocks.meanings: hasta-dvayēna = with two hands | kamalē = the lotus-holding one (Kamalā) | dhārayantīṃ = holding | svalīlayā = effortlessly, as a divine sport | hāra = garland | nūpura = anklet-bells | saṃyuktāṃ = adorned with | lakṣmīṃ devīṃ = Goddess Lakṣmī | vichintayē = I contemplate

contentBlocks.original:
कौशेयपीतवसनामरविन्दनेत्रां
पद्मद्वयाभयवरोद्यतपद्महस्ताम् ।
उद्यच्छतार्कसदृशीं परमाङ्कसंस्थां
ध्यायेद्विधीशनतपादयुगां जनित्रीम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kauśēyapītavasanāmaravindanētrāṃ
padmadvayābhayavarōdyatapadmahastām ।
udyachChatārkasadṛśīṃ paramāṅkasaṃsthāṃ
dhyāyēdvidhīśanatapādayugāṃ janitrīm ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Second meditation verse (Dhyāna 2): One should meditate upon the Mother who wears golden silk (kauśeya), whose eyes are like lotus petals, whose four hands hold two lotuses, the gesture of fearlessness (abhaya), and the boon-granting gesture (vara); who blazes like a hundred rising suns; who rests in the supreme lap (of Viṣṇu); whose lotus feet are served by Brahmā and Indra — she who is the Mother of all.
contentBlocks.meanings: kauśēya = silk (from kauśa, silkworm) | pīta-vasanā = wearing yellow garment | aravinda-nētrā = with lotus eyes | padma-dvaya = two lotuses | abhaya = fearlessness gesture | vara = boon-granting gesture | udyat-śata-arka-sadṛśīṃ = resembling a hundred rising suns | paramāṅka-saṃsthāṃ = seated in the supreme lap (Viṣṇu's) | vidhī-īśa-nata-pāda-yugāṃ = whose two feet are saluted by Brahmā and Indra | janitrīm = the Mother

contentBlocks.original:
पीतवस्त्रां सुवर्णाङ्गीं पद्महस्तद्वायान्विताम् ।
लक्ष्मीं ध्यात्वेति मन्त्रेण स भवेत्पृथिवीपतिः ॥
मातुलुङ्गं गदां खेटं पाणौ पात्रं च बिभ्रती ।
नागं लिङ्गं च योनिं च बिभ्रतीं चैव मूर्धनि ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pītavastrāṃ suvarṇāṅgīṃ padmahastadvāyānvitām ।
lakṣmīṃ dhyātvēti mantrēṇa sa bhavētpṛthivīpatiḥ ॥
mātuluṅgaṃ gadāṃ khēṭaṃ pāṇau pātraṃ cha bibhratī ।
nāgaṃ liṅgaṃ cha yōniṃ cha bibhratīṃ chaiva mūrdhani ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Third meditation verse (Dhyāna 3): One who meditates on Lakṣmī — clad in yellow, golden-bodied, with two lotus-bearing hands, and worships her with this mantra — becomes the lord of the earth. [Second form:] She holds the mātuluṅga fruit, a mace (gadā), a shield (kheṭa), and a vessel (pātra) in her hands — after meditating thus and performing mental worship with the sixteen services, one offers the prayer: "O Lakṣmī holding conch, discus, and mace, of white complexion, O auspicious married goddess — give me your boon, O bestower of all perfections."
contentBlocks.meanings: pīta-vastrāṃ = yellow-clothed | suvarṇāṅgīṃ = golden-bodied | padma-hasta-dvaya = two lotus-bearing hands | dhyātvā = having meditated | iti mantrēṇa = with this mantra | pṛthivī-patiḥ = lord of the earth | mātuluṅga = citron/pomelo fruit | gadā = mace | kheṭa = shield | pātra = vessel/cup | śaṅkha-chakra-gadā-hastē = holding conch, discus, mace | śubhra-varṇē = of white complexion | suvāsinī = blessed married woman | sarva-siddhi-pradāyinī = bestower of all perfections

contentBlocks.original:
इति ध्यात्वा मानसोपचारैः सम्पूज्य
शङ्खचक्रगदाहस्ते शुभ्रवर्णे सुवासिनी ।
मम देहि वरं लक्ष्मीः सर्वसिद्धिप्रदायिनी ।
इति सम्प्रार्थ्य ॐ श्रीं ह्रीं ऐं महालक्ष्म्यै कमलधारिण्यै सिंहवाहिन्यै स्वाहा इति मन्त्रं जप्त्वा पुनः पूर्ववद्धृदयादि षडङ्गन्यासं कृत्वा स्तोत्रं पठेत् । ]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
iti dhyātvā mānasōpachāraiḥ sampūjya
śaṅkhachakragadāhastē śubhravarṇē suvāsinī ।
mama dēhi varaṃ lakṣmīḥ sarvasiddhipradāyinī ।
iti samprārthya ōṃ śrīṃ hrīṃ aiṃ mahālakṣmyai kamaladhāriṇyai siṃhavāhinyai svāhā iti mantraṃ japtvā punaḥ pūrvavaddhṛdayādi ṣaḍaṅganyāsaṃ kṛtvā stōtraṃ paṭhēt । ]
contentBlocks.translation: Having thus meditated and performed mental (mānasopacharaiḥ) worship with the sixteen services, one should pray: "O Lakṣmī holding conch, discus, and mace, of bright white complexion, O auspicious goddess — give me your boon; O bestower of all perfections." Having prayed thus, begin the recitation of Oṃ Śrīṃ Hrīṃ Śrīṃ Kamalālayē Prasīda Prasīda — O Lakṣmī dwelling in the lotus, be gracious, be gracious.

contentBlocks.original:
स्तोत्रम्
वन्दे लक्ष्मीं परमशिवमयीं शुद्धजाम्बूनदाभां
तेजोरूपां कनकवसनां सर्वभूषोज्ज्वलाङ्गीम् ।
बीजापूरं कनककलशं हेमपद्मं दधाना-
-माद्यां शक्तिं सकलजननीं विष्णुवामाङ्कसंस्थाम् ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
stōtram
vandē lakṣmīṃ paramaśivamayīṃ śuddhajāmbūnadābhāṃ
tējōrūpāṃ kanakavasanāṃ sarvabhūṣōjjvalāṅgīm ।
bījāpūraṃ kanakakalaśaṃ hēmapadmaṃ dadhānā-
-mādyāṃ śaktiṃ sakalajananīṃ viṣṇuvāmāṅkasaṃsthām ॥ 1 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 1:] I worship Lakṣmī — supreme, filled with Śiva's auspiciousness, glowing like pure refined gold, the very form of divine radiance, clothed in golden garments, her limbs blazing with all ornaments; who holds the citron (bījāpūra), a golden pitcher (kanaka-kalaśa), a golden lotus (hēma-padma), and grants boons with the fourth hand; who is praised by Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Śiva — I worship this eternal Mahālakṣmī. [Stotram verse 1]
contentBlocks.meanings: vandē = I worship | paramāśiva-mayīṃ = filled with supreme Śiva's auspiciousness | śuddha-jāmbūnada-ābhāṃ = glowing like pure jāmbūnada gold | tējas-rūpāṃ = the very form of radiance | kanaka-vasanāṃ = golden-clothed | sarva-bhūṣā-ujjvala-aṅgīm = limbs blazing with all ornaments | bījāpūra = citron fruit (Citrus medica) | kanaka-kalaśa = golden pitcher | hēma-padma = golden lotus | brahma-viṣṇu-śiva-stutyāṃ = praised by Brahmā, Viṣṇu, and Śiva | sanātanīm = eternal

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीमत्सौभाग्यजननीं स्तौमि लक्ष्मीं सनातनीम् ।
सर्वकामफलावाप्तिसाधनैकसुखावहाम् ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīmatsaubhāgyajananīṃ staumi lakṣmīṃ sanātanīm ।
sarvakāmaphalāvāptisādhanaikasukhāvahām ॥ 2 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 2:] I praise the eternal Lakṣmī, the Mother of all glorious fortune, the one who is the sole means (sādhana) for attaining all desired fruits, who brings supreme happiness to those who approach her.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrīmat-saubhāgya-jananīṃ = mother of glorious good fortune | staumi = I praise | sanātanīm = eternal | sarva-kāma-phala-āvāpti = attainment of all desired fruits | sādhana-eka = the sole means/instrument | sukha-āvahām = one who brings happiness

contentBlocks.original:
स्मरामि नित्यं देवेशि त्वया प्रेरितमानसः ।
त्वदाज्ञां शिरसा धृत्वा भजामि परमेश्वरीम् ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
smarāmi nityaṃ dēvēśi tvayā prēritamānasaḥ ।
tvadājñāṃ śirasā dhṛtvā bhajāmi paramēśvarīm ॥ 3 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 3:] I remember you daily, O Queen of the Gods, my mind prompted by you alone. Bearing your command upon my head, I worship you, the Supreme Goddess.
contentBlocks.meanings: smarāmi = I remember | nityaṃ = daily, always | dēvēśi = O queen of the gods | tvayā prērita-mānasaḥ = my mind prompted/inspired by you | tvad-ājñāṃ = your command | śirasā dhṛtvā = bearing on my head | bhajāmi = I worship | paramēśvarīm = the Supreme Goddess

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तसम्पत्सुखदां महाश्रियं
समस्तसौभाग्यकरीं महाश्रियम् ।
समस्तकल्याणकरीं महाश्रियं
भजाम्यहं ज्ञानकरीं महाश्रियम् ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastasampatsukhadāṃ mahāśriyaṃ
samastasaubhāgyakarīṃ mahāśriyam ।
samastakaḻyāṇakarīṃ mahāśriyaṃ
bhajāmyahaṃ jñānakarīṃ mahāśriyam ॥ 4 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 4:] I worship Mahāśrī who bestows the happiness of all wealth; I worship Mahāśrī who creates all good fortune; I worship Mahāśrī who brings about all auspiciousness; I worship Mahāśrī who grants the knowledge of all things.
contentBlocks.meanings: samasta-sampat-sukha-dāṃ = bestower of the happiness of all wealth | mahāśriyam = the great Śrī | samasta-saubhāgya-karīṃ = creator of all good fortune | samasta-kalyāṇa-karīṃ = performer of all auspiciousness | bhajāmy-ahaṃ = I worship | jñāna-karīṃ = bestower/creator of knowledge

contentBlocks.original:
विज्ञानसम्पत्सुखदां सनातनीं
विचित्रवाग्भूतिकरीं मनोहराम् ।
अनन्तसम्मोदसुखप्रदायिनीं
नमाम्यहं भूतिकरीं हरिप्रियाम् ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vijñānasampatsukhadāṃ sanātanīṃ
vichitravāgbhūtikarīṃ manōharām ।
anantasammōdasukhapradāyinīṃ
namāmyahaṃ bhūtikarīṃ haripriyām ॥ 5 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 5:] I bow to the eternal one who bestows the happiness of the wealth of spiritual knowledge; who creates wonderful eloquence and prosperity; who is mind-captivating; who grants infinite, ever-refreshing happiness — I bow to the glory-giving beloved of Hari.
contentBlocks.meanings: vijñāna-sampat-sukha-dāṃ = bestower of the happiness of the wealth of vijñāna (spiritual knowledge) | sanātanīṃ = eternal | vichitra-vāg-bhūti-karīṃ = creator of wonderful eloquence and prosperity | manō-harām = mind-captivating | ananta-sammōda-sukha-pradāyinīṃ = giver of infinite ever-new bliss | bhūti-karīṃ = creator of prosperity | hari-priyām = the beloved of Hari (Viṣṇu) | namāmi = I bow

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तभूतान्तरसंस्थिता त्वं
समस्तभोक्त्रीश्वरि विश्वरूपे ।
तन्नास्ति यत्त्वद्व्यतिरिक्तवस्तु
त्वत्पादपद्मं प्रणमाम्यहं श्रीः ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastabhūtāntarasaṃsthitā tvaṃ
samastabhōktrīśvari viśvarūpē ।
tannāsti yattvadvyatiriktavastu
tvatpādapadmaṃ praṇamāmyahaṃ śrīḥ ॥ 6 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 6:] You dwell within all beings; you are the enjoyer and the governor of all, O goddess of universal form. There is nothing that exists apart from you. I bow to your lotus feet, O Śrī.
contentBlocks.meanings: samasta-bhūta-antara-saṃsthitā = dwelling within all beings | samasta-bhōktri-īśvari = the enjoyer and ruler of all | viśva-rūpē = O goddess of universal form | tat na asti yat tvad-vyatirikta-vastu = there is no object that exists apart from you | tvatpāda-padmaṃ praṇamāmi = I bow to your lotus feet | ahaṃ śrīḥ = O Śrī

contentBlocks.original:
दारिद्र्य दुःखौघतमोपहन्त्री
त्वत्पादपद्मं मयि सन्निधत्स्व ।
दीनार्तिविच्छेदनहेतुभूतैः
कृपाकटाक्षैरभिषिञ्च मां श्रीः ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dāridrya duḥkhaughatamōpahantrī
tvatpādapadmaṃ mayi sannidhatsva ।
dīnārtivichChēdanahētubhūtaiḥ
kṛpākaṭākṣairabhiṣiñcha māṃ śrīḥ ॥ 7 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 7:] You are the destroyer of the great dark flood of poverty-born suffering. Let your lotus feet abide in me. O Śrī — consecrate me with the glances of your grace, those glances which are the very cause of cutting the afflictions of the humble and poor.
contentBlocks.meanings: dāridrya = poverty | duḥkha-ogha = flood of suffering | tamas-upahantrī = destroyer of darkness | tvatpāda-padmaṃ = your lotus feet | mayi sannidhatsva = let them be present in me | dīna-ārti-vichchhēdana-hētu-bhūtaiḥ = those which are the cause of cutting the afflictions of the humble | kṛpā-kaṭākṣaiḥ = with the glances of grace/compassion | abhiṣiñcha māṃ = consecrate/sprinkle me | śrīḥ = O Śrī

contentBlocks.original:
अम्ब प्रसीद करुणासुधयार्द्रदृष्ट्या
मां त्वत्कृपाद्रविणगेहमिमं कुरुष्व ।
आलोकय प्रणतहृद्गतशोकहन्त्री
त्वत्पादपद्मयुगलं प्रणमाम्यहं श्रीः ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amba prasīda karuṇāsudhayārdradṛṣṭyā
māṃ tvatkṛpādraviṇagēhamimaṃ kuruṣva ।
ālōkaya praṇatahṛdgataśōkahantrī
tvatpādapadmayugaḻaṃ praṇamāmyahaṃ śrīḥ ॥ 8 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 8:] O Mother, be gracious; with your compassion-nectar-moistened gaze, make this home of mine a treasury of your grace. Look upon me, O destroyer of the sorrow lodged in the hearts of those who bow to you — I bow to the pair of your lotus feet, O Śrī.
contentBlocks.meanings: amba prasīda = O Mother, be gracious | karuṇā-sudhayā = with compassion-nectar | ardra-dṛṣṭyā = with a moistened/tender gaze | māṃ tvat-kṛpā-draviṇa-gēham imaṃ kuruṣva = make this [body/home] mine a treasury of your grace | ālōkaya = look upon | praṇata-hṛd-gata-śōka-hantrī = destroyer of sorrow in the hearts of the bowed | tvatpāda-padma-yugaḻaṃ praṇamāmi = I bow to the pair of your lotus feet

contentBlocks.original:
शान्त्यै नमोऽस्तु शरणागतरक्षणायै
कान्त्यै नमोऽस्तु कमनीयगुणाश्रयायै ।
क्षान्त्यै नमोऽस्तु दुरितक्षयकारणायै
दात्र्यै नमोऽस्तु धनधान्यसमृद्धिदायै ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śāntyai namō'stu śaraṇāgatarakṣaṇāyai
kāntyai namō'stu kamanīyaguṇāśrayāyai ।
kṣāntyai namō'stu duritakṣayakāraṇāyai
dātryai namō'stu dhanadhānyasamṛddhidāyai ॥ 9 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 9 — the Namas:] Salutation to Śānti (Peace), the protector of those who seek refuge. Salutation to Kānti (Radiance), the abode of desirable qualities. Salutation to Kṣānti (Forbearance), the cause of the destruction of sins. Salutation to Dātṛ (the Giver), the bestower of wealth and grain in abundance.
contentBlocks.meanings: śāntyai = to Peace | śaraṇāgata-rakṣaṇāyai = to the protector of those who seek refuge | kāntyai = to Radiance | kamanīya-guṇa-āśrayāyai = to the abode of beautiful qualities | kṣāntyai = to Forbearance/Patience | durita-kṣaya-kāraṇāyai = to the cause of destruction of sins | dātryai = to the Giver | dhana-dhānya-samṛddhi-dāyai = to the bestower of wealth and grain abundance

contentBlocks.original:
शक्त्यै नमोऽस्तु शशिशेखरसंस्तुतायै
रत्यै नमोऽस्तु रजनीकरसोदरायै ।
भक्त्यै नमोऽस्तु भवसागरतारकायै
मत्यै नमोऽस्तु मधुसूदनवल्लभायै ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śaktyai namō'stu śaśiśēkharasaṃstutāyai
ratyai namō'stu rajanīkarasōdarāyai ।
bhaktyai namō'stu bhavasāgaratārakāyai
matyai namō'stu madhusūdanavallabhāyai ॥ 10 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 10:] Salutation to Śakti (Power), praised by the crescent-moon-bearing Śiva. Salutation to Rati (Joy), the sister of the moon. Salutation to Bhakti (Devotion), the ferryman across the ocean of worldly existence. Salutation to Mati (Intellect), the beloved of Madhusūdana (Viṣṇu).
contentBlocks.meanings: śaktyai = to Power | śaśi-śēkhara-saṃstutyāyai = praised by the crescent-moon-bearing one (Śiva) | ratyai = to Joy/Rati | rajanī-kara-sōdarāyai = sister of the moon | bhaktyai = to Devotion | bhava-sāgara-tārakāyai = the one who enables crossing the ocean of worldly existence | matyai = to Intellect | madhu-sūdana-vallabhāyai = the beloved of Madhusūdana

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्म्यै नमोऽस्तु शुभलक्षणलक्षितायै
सिद्ध्यै नमोऽस्तु शिवसिद्धसुपूजितायै ।
धृत्यै नमोऽस्त्वमितदुर्गतिभञ्जनायै
गत्यै नमोऽस्तु वरसद्गतिदायिकायै ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmyai namō'stu śubhalakṣaṇalakṣitāyai
siddhyai namō'stu śivasiddhasupūjitāyai ।
dhṛtyai namō'stvamitadurgatibhañjanāyai
gatyai namō'stu varasadgatidāyikāyai ॥ 11 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 11:] Salutation to Lakṣmī, adorned with auspicious marks. Salutation to Siddhi (Accomplishment), well-worshipped by the Śiva-perfected ones. Salutation to Dhṛti (Courage), the destroyer of unlimited adversity. Salutation to Gati (the Path), the bestower of the excellent final destination.
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmyai = to Lakṣmī | śubha-lakṣaṇa-lakṣitāyai = adorned with auspicious marks | siddhyai = to Accomplishment | śiva-siddha-supūjitāyai = well-worshipped by Śiva and the perfected ones | dhṛtyai = to Courage/Steadiness | amita-durgati-bhañjanāyai = destroyer of limitless adversity | gatyai = to the Path/Direction | vara-sad-gati-dāyai = bestower of the excellent good destination (mokṣa)

contentBlocks.original:
देव्यै नमोऽस्तु दिवि देवगणार्चितायै
भूत्यै नमोऽस्तु भुवनार्तिविनाशनायै ।
धात्र्यै नमोऽस्तु धरणीधरवल्लभायै
पुष्ट्यै नमोऽस्तु पुरुषोत्तमवल्लभायै ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvyai namō'stu divi dēvagaṇārchitāyai
bhūtyai namō'stu bhuvanārtivināśanāyai ।
dhātryai namō'stu dharaṇīdharavallabhāyai
puṣṭyai namō'stu puruṣōttamavallabhāyai ॥ 12 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 12:] Salutation to Dēvī, worshipped by the assembled gods in heaven. Salutation to Bhūti (Prosperity), the destroyer of the suffering of all worlds. Salutation to Dhātṛ (the Sustainer), the beloved of the earth-bearer (Viṣṇu). Salutation to Puṣṭi (Nourishment), the beloved of Puruṣōttama (the Supreme Person).
contentBlocks.meanings: dēvyai = to the Goddess | divi dēva-gaṇa-architāyai = worshipped by the assembly of gods in heaven | bhūtyai = to Prosperity | bhuvana-ārti-vināśanāyai = destroyer of the suffering of all worlds | dhātryai = to the Sustainer/Nourisher | dharaṇī-dhara-vallabhāyai = beloved of the earth-bearer (Viṣṇu who bears the earth) | puṣṭyai = to Nourishment | puruṣōttama-vallabhāyai = beloved of Puruṣōttama

contentBlocks.original:
सुतीव्रदारिद्र्यविदुःखहन्त्र्यै
नमोऽस्तु ते सर्वभयापहन्त्र्यै ।
श्रीविष्णुवक्षःस्थलसंस्थितायै
नमो नमः सर्वविभूतिदायै ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sutīvradāridryaviduḥkhahantryai
namō'stu tē sarvabhayāpahantryai ।
śrīviṣṇuvakṣaḥsthalasaṃsthitāyai
namō namaḥ sarvavibhūtidāyai ॥ 13 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 13:] Salutation, salutation to you, O destroyer of the anguish of fierce poverty; O destroyer of all fears; O you who are seated on the chest of Śrī Viṣṇu — salutation, salutation to the bestower of all splendour and glory.
contentBlocks.meanings: sutīvra-dāridrya-viduḥkha-hantrī = destroyer of the suffering born of fierce poverty | namō'stu = salutation | sarvabhaya-apahantrī = remover of all fears | śrī-viṣṇu-vakṣaḥ-sthala-saṃsthitāyai = she who abides on the chest of Śrī Viṣṇu | sarva-vibhūti-dāyai = bestower of all glories and splendours

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु लक्ष्मीर्लक्षणालङ्कृताङ्गी
जयतु जयतु पद्मा पद्मसद्माभिवन्द्या ।
जयतु जयतु विद्या विष्णुवामाङ्कसंस्था
जयतु जयतु सम्यक्सर्वसम्पत्करी श्रीः ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu lakṣmīrlakṣaṇālaṅkṛtāṅgī
jayatu jayatu padmā padmasadmābhivandyā ।
jayatu jayatu vidyā viṣṇuvāmāṅkasaṃsthā
jayatu jayatu samyaksarvasampatkarī śrīḥ ॥ 14 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 14 — the Jaya (Victory) verses:] Victory, victory to Lakṣmī, whose limbs are adorned with the marks of all auspiciousness! Victory, victory to Padmā, honoured by the dwellers of the lotus-house (Brahmā)! Victory, victory to Vidyā, seated at the left side of Viṣṇu! Victory, victory to her who is filled with all wealth and prosperity at all times!
contentBlocks.meanings: jayatu = may she be victorious/praised | lakṣmīr = Lakṣmī | lakṣaṇa-alaṅkṛta-aṅgī = limbs adorned with auspicious marks | padmā = Padmā (lotus-born Lakṣmī) | padma-sadma-abhivandyā = honoured by the dweller in the lotus house (Brahmā) | vidyā = Knowledge | viṣṇu-vāma-aṅka-saṃsthā = seated on Viṣṇu's left side/lap | samyak-sarva-sampat-prasannā = filled with all wealth and grace at all times

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु देवी देवसङ्घाभिपूज्या
जयतु जयतु भद्रा भार्गवी भाग्यरूपा ।
जयतु जयतु नित्या निर्मलज्ञानवेद्या
जयतु जयतु सत्या सर्वभूतान्तरस्था ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu dēvī dēvasaṅghābhipūjyā
jayatu jayatu bhadrā bhārgavī bhāgyarūpā ।
jayatu jayatu nityā nirmalajñānavēdyā
jayatu jayatu satyā sarvabhūtāntarasthā ॥ 15 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 15:] Victory, victory to the Goddess, worshipped by the assembly of divine beings! Victory, victory to Bhadrā, Bhārgavī, the very form of good fortune! Victory, victory to the eternal one, known through pure knowledge! Victory, victory to the True one, the inner soul of all beings!
contentBlocks.meanings: dēvī = Goddess | dēva-saṅgha-abhipūjyā = worshipped by the assembly of gods | bhadrā = the auspicious/kind one | bhārgavī = of Bhṛgu's lineage (daughter of Bhṛgu) | bhāgya-rūpā = very form of destiny/fortune | nityā = eternal | nirmala-jñāna-vēdyā = known through pure knowledge | satyā = Truth | sarva-bhūta-antarasthā = dwelling in the interior of all beings

contentBlocks.original:
जयतु जयतु रम्या रत्नगर्भान्तरस्था
जयतु जयतु शुद्धा शुद्धजाम्बूनदाभा ।
जयतु जयतु कान्ता कान्तिमद्भासिताङ्गी
जयतु जयतु शान्ता शीघ्रमागच्छ सौम्ये ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
jayatu jayatu ramyā ratnagarbhāntarasthā
jayatu jayatu śuddhā śuddhajāmbūnadābhā ।
jayatu jayatu kāntā kāntimadbhāsitāṅgī
jayatu jayatu śāntā śīghramāgachCha saumyē ॥ 16 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 16:] Victory, victory to the beautiful one, dwelling within the gem-ocean! Victory, victory to the pure one, glowing like pure refined gold! Victory, victory to the radiant one, whose limbs blaze with luminosity! Victory, victory to the serene one — may she quickly come to me!
contentBlocks.meanings: ramyā = beautiful, delightful | ratna-garbha-antara-sthā = dwelling within the gem-ocean/gem-womb | śuddhā = pure | śuddha-jāmbūnada-ābhā = glowing like pure jāmbūnada gold | kāntā = radiant, luminous | kāntimat-bhāsita-aṅgī = limbs illumined with radiant lustre | śāntā = serene, tranquil | śīghram āgachcha = may she come quickly [to me]

contentBlocks.original:
यस्याः कलायाः कमलोद्भवाद्या
रुद्राश्च शक्र प्रमुखाश्च देवाः ।
जीवन्ति सर्वेऽपि सशक्तयस्ते
प्रभुत्वमाप्ताः परमायुषस्ते ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasyāḥ kalāyāḥ kamalōdbhavādyā
rudrāścha śakra pramukhāścha dēvāḥ ।
jīvanti sarvē'pi saśaktayastē
prabhutvamāptāḥ paramāyuṣastē ॥ 17 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 17:] By whose kalā (divine portion) even Brahmā born from the lotus, Rudra, Indra, and all the foremost gods — along with their own powers (śaktis) — live, gain sovereignty, and attain the full span of life.
contentBlocks.meanings: yasyāḥ kalāyāḥ = by whose divine portion/fraction | kamalōdbhavādyā = beginning with Brahmā born from the lotus | rudrāścha śakra-pramukhāścha dēvāḥ = Rudra and Indra and other foremost gods | jīvanti = live | sarve api = all also | saśaktayaḥ = together with their own powers | tē = they | prabhutvam āptāḥ = having gained sovereignty | parama-āyuṣaḥ = of the full/supreme span of life

contentBlocks.original:
लिलेख निटिले विधिर्मम लिपिं विसृज्यान्तरं
त्वया विलिखितव्यमेतदिति तत्फलप्राप्तये ।
तदन्तरफलेस्फुटं कमलवासिनी श्रीरिमां
समर्पय समुद्रिकां सकलभाग्यसंसूचिकाम् ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lilēkha niṭilē vidhirmama lipiṃ visṛjyāntaraṃ
tvayā vilikhitavyamētaditi tatphalaprāptayē ।
tadantaraphalēsphuṭaṃ kamalavāsinī śrīrimāṃ
samarpaya samudrikāṃ sakalabhāgyasaṃsūchikām ॥ 18 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 18:] Brahmā wrote my fate upon my forehead, leaving a gap in the middle [of what was written], saying: "This much [result] you must write [and fill]." For the attainment of that intermediate fruit, O Kamala-dweller, O Śrī — grant me that fruit; come forward smiling, O Ramā!
contentBlocks.meanings: lilēkha = wrote | niṭilē = on the forehead | vidhiḥ = Brahmā (the fate-ordainer) | mama lipiṃ = my fate-inscription | visṛjya = leaving/releasing | antaraṃ = a gap in the middle | tvayā vilikhita-vyam ētad = this must be written/completed by you | tat-phala-prāptayē = for the attainment of that fruit | kamala-vāsinī śrīḥ = O Lakṣmī dwelling in the lotus | samarpaya = grant/offer | samudra-rāja-tanaye = O daughter of the ocean-king | ramā = Ramā (Lakṣmī)

contentBlocks.original:
कलया ते यथा देवि जीवन्ति सचराचराः ।
तथा सम्पत्करे लक्ष्मि सर्वदा सम्प्रसीद मे ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalayā tē yathā dēvi jīvanti sacharācharāḥ ।
tathā sampatkarē lakṣmi sarvadā samprasīda mē ॥ 19 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 19:] Just as all moving and unmoving beings live by your kalā (divine portion), O Goddess — so too, O Lakṣmī whose hands hold all prosperity — may you always be gracious to me.
contentBlocks.meanings: kalayā tē = by your kalā | yathā dēvi = just as, O Goddess | jīvanti = live, exist | sacharācharāḥ = all moving and non-moving beings | tathā sampat-karē lakṣmi = likewise, O Lakṣmī whose hands hold/make prosperity | sarvadā = always | samprasīda = be fully gracious | mē = to me

contentBlocks.original:
यथा विष्णुर्ध्रुवे नित्यं स्वकलां सन्न्यवेशयत् ।
तथैव स्वकलां लक्ष्मि मयि सम्यक् समर्पय ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā viṣṇurdhruvē nityaṃ svakalāṃ sannyavēśayat ।
tathaiva svakalāṃ lakṣmi mayi samyak samarpaya ॥ 20 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 20:] Just as Viṣṇu permanently infused his own divine portion (kalā) into Dhruva, O Lakṣmī — so too, properly and completely infuse your own divine portion into me.
contentBlocks.meanings: yathā viṣṇuḥ = just as Viṣṇu | dhruvē = in Dhruva (the Pole Star devotee) | nityaṃ = permanently | sva-kalāṃ = his own divine portion | sannyavēśayat = infused/installed | tathā eva = likewise | sva-kalāṃ lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī, your own divine portion | mayi = in me | samyak = properly, fully | samarpaya = infuse/bestow

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वसौख्यप्रदे देवि भक्तानामभयप्रदे ।
अचलां कुरु यत्नेन कलां मयि निवेशिताम् ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvasaukhyapradē dēvi bhaktānāmabhayapradē ।
achalāṃ kuru yatnēna kalāṃ mayi nivēśitām ॥ 21 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 21:] O bestower of all happiness, O granter of fearlessness to your devotees — with effort, make the kalā (divine portion) that has been placed in me steadfast and unwavering.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-saukhya-pradē dēvi = O Goddess who bestows all happiness | bhaktānām-abhaya-pradē = O granter of fearlessness to devotees | achalāṃ = immovable, unwavering | kuru = make | yatnēna = with effort/care | kalāṃ = the divine portion | mayi = in me | nivēśitām = that which has been placed/installed

contentBlocks.original:
मुदास्तां मत्फाले परमपदलक्ष्मीः स्फुटकला
सदा वैकुण्ठश्रीर्निवसतु कला मे नयनयोः ।
वसेत्सत्ये लोके मम वचसि लक्ष्मीर्वरकला
श्रियः श्वेतद्वीपे निवसतु कला मे स्वकरयोः ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mudāstāṃ matphālē paramapadalakṣmīḥ sphuṭakalā
sadā vaikuṇṭhaśrīrnivasatu kalā mē nayanayōḥ ।
vasētsatyē lōkē mama vachasi lakṣmīrvarakalā
śriyaḥ śvētadvīpē nivasatu kalā mē svakarayōḥ ॥ 22 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 22:] May the radiant kalā of Paramapada-Lakṣmī shine joyfully upon my forehead; may the kalā of Vaikuṇṭha-Śrī ever dwell in my eyes; may the excellent kalā of Lakṣmī abide in my speech in the Satya-loka; may the white-lotus-seated kalā of Śrī forever abide in me.
contentBlocks.meanings: mudā astāṃ = may she joyfully abide | mat-phālē = on my forehead | paramapada-lakṣmīḥ sphuṭa-kalā = the manifest kalā of Lakṣmī of the supreme state | sadā vaikuṇṭha-śrīḥ nivasatu = may the Vaikuṇṭha-Śrī ever dwell | kalā mē nayanayoḥ = her kalā in my eyes | vasēt satyē lōkē = may she dwell in the Satya-realm | mama vachasi = in my speech | lakṣmīḥ vara-kalā = excellent kalā of Lakṣmī | śriyaḥ śvēta-ambuja-saṃsthitā kalā = the kalā of Śrī seated on the white lotus

contentBlocks.original:
तावन्नित्यं ममाङ्गेषु क्षीराब्धौ श्रीकला वसेत् ।
सूर्याचन्द्रमसौ यावद्यावल्लक्ष्मीपतिः श्रियाः ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tāvannityaṃ mamāṅgēṣu kṣīrābdhau śrīkalā vasēt ।
sūryāchandramasau yāvadyāvallakṣmīpatiḥ śriyāḥ ॥ 23 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 23:] For as long as the sun and moon exist, for as long as Viṣṇu and Lakṣmī herself exist — may the kalā of the Milk-Ocean Śrī dwell eternally in my limbs.
contentBlocks.meanings: tāvat nityaṃ = for that long, permanently | mamāṅgēṣu = in my limbs | kṣīrābdhau śrī-kalā vasēt = may the kalā of Śrī of the milk-ocean dwell | sūryā-chandramasau yāvat = as long as sun and moon | yāvat lakṣmī-patiḥ = as long as Lakṣmī's Lord (Viṣṇu) | śriyāḥ = of Śrī (Lakṣmī)

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वमङ्गलसम्पूर्णा सर्वैश्वर्यसमन्विता ।
आद्यादि श्रीर्महालक्ष्मी त्वत्कला मयि तिष्ठतु ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvamaṅgaḻasampūrṇā sarvaiśvaryasamanvitā ।
ādyādi śrīrmahālakṣmī tvatkalā mayi tiṣṭhatu ॥ 24 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 24:] O Ādi Śrī Mahālakṣmī, you who are filled with all auspiciousness, endowed with all sovereignty — may your divine portion (kalā) stand firmly in me.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-maṅgala-sampūrṇā = filled/complete with all auspiciousness | sarva-aiśvarya-samanvitā = endowed with all sovereignty | ādyādi śrīḥ mahā-lakṣmī = Ādi Śrī Mahālakṣmī | tvat-kalā mayi tiṣṭhatu = may your divine portion stand/abide in me

contentBlocks.original:
अज्ञानतिमिरं हन्तुं शुद्धज्ञानप्रकाशिका ।
सर्वैश्वर्यप्रदा मेऽस्तु त्वत्कला मयि संस्थिता ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ajñānatimiraṃ hantuṃ śuddhajñānaprakāśikā ।
sarvaiśvaryapradā mē'stu tvatkalā mayi saṃsthitā ॥ 25 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 25:] To destroy the darkness of ignorance, shining as the illuminator of pure knowledge, bestowing all sovereignty — may your kalā, installed in me, be so for me.
contentBlocks.meanings: ajñāna-timiraṃ hantuṃ = to destroy the darkness of ignorance | śuddha-jñāna-prakāśikā = illuminatrix of pure knowledge | sarvaiśvarya-pradā = bestower of all sovereignty | mē astu = may she be for me | tvat-kalā = your divine portion | mayi saṃsthitā = installed in me

contentBlocks.original:
अलक्ष्मीं हरतु क्षिप्रं तमः सूर्यप्रभा यथा ।
वितनोतु मम श्रेयस्त्वत्कला मयि संस्थिता ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
alakṣmīṃ haratu kṣipraṃ tamaḥ sūryaprabhā yathā ।
vitanōtu mama śrēyastvatkaḻā mayi saṃsthitā ॥ 26 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 26:] Just as the sun's radiance swiftly dispels darkness, may your kalā installed in me swiftly remove misfortune (alakṣmī) and spread my well-being.
contentBlocks.meanings: alakṣmīṃ haratu kṣipraṃ = may she quickly remove alakṣmī (misfortune, the anti-Lakṣmī) | tamaḥ sūrya-prabhā yathā = just as sunlight removes darkness | vitanōtu mama śrēyaḥ = may she expand/spread my welfare | tvat-kalā mayi saṃsthitā = your kalā installed in me

contentBlocks.original:
ऐश्वर्यमङ्गलोत्पत्तिस्त्वत्कलायां निधीयते ।
मयि तस्मात्कृतार्थोऽस्मि पात्रमस्मि स्थितेस्तव ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aiśvaryamaṅgaḻōtpattistvatkalāyāṃ nidhīyatē ।
mayi tasmātkṛtārthō'smi pātramasmi sthitēstava ॥ 27 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 27:] The arising of sovereignty and auspiciousness is stored in your kalā. Therefore I am fulfilled; I am worthy of your abiding presence, O Lakṣmī.
contentBlocks.meanings: aiśvarya-maṅgala-utpattis = the arising of sovereignty and auspiciousness | tvat-kalāyāṃ nidhīyatē = is stored/deposited in your divine portion | mayi = in me | tasmāt = therefore | kṛtārthaḥ asmi = I am fulfilled/accomplished | pātram asmi sthitēstava = I am a worthy vessel for your abiding presence

contentBlocks.original:
भवदावेशभाग्यार्हो भाग्यवानस्मि भार्गवि ।
त्वत्प्रसादात्पवित्रोऽहं लोकमातर्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavadāvēśabhāgyārhō bhāgyavānasmi bhārgavi ।
tvatprasādātpavitrō'haṃ lōkamātarnamō'stu tē ॥ 28 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 28:] I am blessed to be worthy of receiving your descent (bhavadāvēśa), O Bhārgavī. By your grace I am purified, O World-Mother — salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhavad-āvēśa-bhāgya-arhaḥ = worthy of the fortune of your descent/possession | bhāgyavān asmi = I am fortunate | bhārgavi = O Bhārgavī (daughter of Bhṛgu, Lakṣmī) | tvat-prasādāt = by your grace | pavitraḥ aham = I am purified | lōka-mātaḥ = O World-Mother | namō'stu tē = salutation to you

contentBlocks.original:
पुनासि मां त्वत्कलयैव यस्मा-
-दतः समागच्छ ममाग्रतस्त्वम् ।
परं पदं श्रीर्भव सुप्रसन्ना
मय्यच्युतेन प्रविशादिलक्ष्मीः ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
punāsi māṃ tvatkalayaiva yasmā-
-dataḥ samāgachCha mamāgratastvam ।
paraṃ padaṃ śrīrbhava suprasannā
mayyachyutēna praviśādilakṣmīḥ ॥ 29 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 29:] Since you purify me by your own kalā, come before me. O Śrī, O Ādi-Lakṣmī — be supremely gracious; enter into me together with Achyuta (Viṣṇu) as the Supreme State.
contentBlocks.meanings: punāsi māṃ tvatkalayaiva = you purify me by your own kalā | yasmāt = since, because | ataḥ samāgachcha = therefore come | mamāgrataḥ = before me | paraṃ padaṃ śrīḥ = O Śrī who is the Supreme State | bhava su-prasannā = be most gracious | mayi achyutēna praviśa = enter into me with Achyuta | ādi-lakṣmīḥ = O Ādi-Lakṣmī

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीवैकुण्ठस्थिते लक्ष्मि समागच्छ ममाग्रतः ।
नारायणेन सह मां कृपादृष्ट्याऽवलोकय ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīvaikuṇṭhasthitē lakṣmi samāgachCha mamāgrataḥ ।
nārāyaṇēna saha māṃ kṛpādṛṣṭyā'valōkaya ॥ 30 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 30:] O Lakṣmī dwelling in Śrī Vaikuṇṭha — come before me; look upon me who bow to you, with Nārāyaṇa, with your compassionate gaze.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-vaikuṇṭha-sthitē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī dwelling in Śrī Vaikuṇṭha | samāgachcha = come | mamāgrataḥ = before me | nārāyaṇēna saha = together with Nārāyaṇa | māṃ = me | kṛpā-dṛṣṭyā = with your gracious gaze | avalōkaya = look upon/behold

contentBlocks.original:
सत्यलोकस्थिते लक्ष्मि त्वं ममागच्छ सन्निधिम् ।
वासुदेवेन सहिता प्रसीद वरदा भव ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
satyalōkasthitē lakṣmi tvaṃ mamāgachCha sannidhim ।
vāsudēvēna sahitā prasīda varadā bhava ॥ 31 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 31:] O Lakṣmī dwelling in Satya-loka — come near me; together with Vāsudeva, be gracious to me; be a bestower of boons.
contentBlocks.meanings: satya-lōka-sthitē = dwelling in Satya-loka (the highest of the cosmic realms) | lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | tvaṃ mamāgachcha sannidhim = come to my proximity | vāsudēvēna sahitā = together with Vāsudeva (Kṛṣṇa/Viṣṇu) | prasīda = be gracious | varadā bhava = be a bestower of boons

contentBlocks.original:
श्वेतद्वीपस्थिते लक्ष्मि शीघ्रमागच्छ सुव्रते ।
विष्णुना सहिते देवि जगन्मातः प्रसीद मे ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śvētadvīpasthitē lakṣmi śīghramāgachCha suvratē ।
viṣṇunā sahitē dēvi jaganmātaḥ prasīda mē ॥ 32 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 32:] O Lakṣmī dwelling on the White Island (Śvētadvīpa), come quickly, O faithful one; O Goddess, together with Viṣṇu — O mother of all worlds, be gracious to me.
contentBlocks.meanings: śvētadvīpa-sthitē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī dwelling on the White Island | śīghram āgachcha = come quickly | su-vratē = O faithful/virtuous one | viṣṇunā sahitē dēvi = O Goddess together with Viṣṇu | jagat-mātaḥ = O mother of all worlds | prasīda mē = be gracious to me

contentBlocks.original:
क्षीराम्बुधिस्थिते लक्ष्मि समागच्छ समाधवा ।
त्वत्कृपादृष्टिसुधया सततं मां विलोकय ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣīrāmbudhisthitē lakṣmi samāgachCha samādhavā ।
tvatkṛpādṛṣṭisudhayā satataṃ māṃ vilōkaya ॥ 33 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 33:] O Lakṣmī dwelling in the Milk-Ocean — together with Mādhava, come; by the nectar of your compassionate gaze, look upon me always.
contentBlocks.meanings: kṣīra-ambudi-sthitē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī dwelling in the milk-ocean | samāgachcha = come | samādhavā = together with Mādhava (Viṣṇu, husband of Mā = Lakṣmī) | tvat-kṛpā-dṛṣṭi-sudhayā = by the nectar of your compassionate gaze | satataṃ = always | māṃ vilōkaya = look upon me

contentBlocks.original:
रत्नगर्भस्थिते लक्ष्मि परिपूर्णे हिरण्मये ।
समागच्छ समागच्छ स्थित्वाऽऽशु पुरतो मम ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ratnagarbhasthitē lakṣmi paripūrṇē hiraṇmayē ।
samāgachCha samāgachCha sthitvā''śu puratō mama ॥ 34 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 34:] O Lakṣmī dwelling in the gem-womb (ratnagarbha), full and golden — come, come! Stand before me at once.
contentBlocks.meanings: ratna-garbha-sthitē = dwelling in the gem-womb (the interior of the earth) | lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | paripūrṇē = in the fully complete one | hiraṇmayē = in the golden one | samāgachcha samāgachcha = come, come (urgent repetition) | sthitvā āśu puratō mama = having stood quickly before me

contentBlocks.original:
स्थिरा भव महालक्ष्मि निश्चला भव निर्मले ।
प्रसन्ने कमले देवि प्रसन्नहृदया भव ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sthirā bhava mahālakṣmi niśchalā bhava nirmalē ।
prasannē kamalē dēvi prasannahṛdayā bhava ॥ 35 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 35:] O Mahālakṣmī, become steadfast; become immovable, O immaculate one; O gracious lotus-goddess — become settled here with a cheerful heart; be gracious.
contentBlocks.meanings: sthirā bhava = become steadfast/stable | mahā-lakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī | niśchalā bhava = become immovable | nirmalē = O immaculate one | prasannē kamalē dēvi = O gracious lotus-goddess | prasanna-hṛdayā bhava = become settled with a gracious heart

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीधरे श्रीमहाभूते त्वदन्तःस्थं महानिधिम् ।
शीघ्रमुद्धृत्य पुरतः प्रदर्शय समर्पय ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīdharē śrīmahābhūtē tvadantaḥsthaṃ mahānidhim ।
śīghramuddhṛtya purataḥ pradarśaya samarpaya ॥ 36 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 36:] O Śrīdharā (bearer of Śrī), O great Being of glory — quickly draw out and show me the great treasure that is hidden within you.
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-dharē = O bearer of Śrī (Viṣṇu, or the earth as bearer of Lakṣmī) | śrī-mahā-bhūtē = O great Being of glory | tvad-antaḥ-sthaṃ = that which is within you | mahā-nidhim = the great treasure | śīghram = quickly | uddhṛtya = having drawn out | purataḥ = before | pradarśaya = show | samarpaya = offer/give

contentBlocks.original:
वसुन्धरे श्रीवसुधे वसुदोग्ध्रि कृपामये ।
त्वत्कुक्षिगतसर्वस्वं शीघ्रं मे सम्प्रदर्शय ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vasundharē śrīvasudhē vasudōgdhri kṛpāmayē ।
tvatkukṣigatasarvasvaṃ śīghraṃ mē sampradarśaya ॥ 37 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 37:] O Vasundharā, O Śrī Vasudhā, O compassionate giver of wealth, quickly show and reveal to me all the riches that are within your womb.
contentBlocks.meanings: vasundharē = O earth (vasundhara = bearer of wealth) | śrī-vasudhē = O Śrī Vasudhā (earth) | vasu-dōgdhrī = milk-giver of wealth (dōgdhrī = milker/provider) | kṛpā-mayē = O compassionate one | tvat-kukṣi-gata-sarvasvaṃ = all the riches that have entered/are within your womb | śīghraṃ = quickly | mē sampradarśaya = show/reveal to me fully

contentBlocks.original:
विष्णुप्रिये रत्नगर्भे समस्तफलदे शिवे ।
त्वद्गर्भगतहेमादीन् सम्प्रदर्शय दर्शय ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
viṣṇupriyē ratnagarbhē samastaphaladē śivē ।
tvadgarbhagatahēmādīn sampradarśaya darśaya ॥ 38 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 38:] O beloved of Viṣṇu, O gem-wombed one, O bestower of all fruits, O auspicious one — show me, show me the gold and other riches within your womb.
contentBlocks.meanings: viṣṇu-priyē = O beloved of Viṣṇu | ratna-garbhē = O gem-wombed one | samasta-phala-dē = O bestower of all fruits | śivē = O auspicious one | tvad-garbha-gata-hēmādīn = the gold and other riches within your womb | sampradarśaya darśaya = show me, show me (urgent repetition)

contentBlocks.original:
रसातलगते लक्ष्मि शीघ्रमागच्छ मे पुरः ।
न जाने परमं रूपं मातर्मे सम्प्रदर्शय ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rasātalagatē lakṣmi śīghramāgachCha mē puraḥ ।
na jānē paramaṃ rūpaṃ mātarmē sampradarśaya ॥ 39 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 39:] O Lakṣmī who has gone to the netherworld (rasātala) — come before me quickly. I do not know your supreme form, O Mother — show it to me.
contentBlocks.meanings: rasātala-gatē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī who has gone to the rasātala (netherworld/subterranean realm) | śīghram āgachcha = come quickly | mē puraḥ = before me | na jānē = I do not know | paramaṃ rūpaṃ = your supreme form | mātar = O Mother | mē sampradarśaya = show me fully

contentBlocks.original:
आविर्भव मनोवेगाच्छीघ्रमागच्छ मे पुरः ।
मा वत्स भैरिहेत्युक्त्वा कामं गौरिव रक्ष माम् ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
āvirbhava manōvēgāchChīghramāgachCha mē puraḥ ।
mā vatsa bhairihētyuktvā kāmaṃ gauriva rakṣa mām ॥ 40 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 40:] Appear at the speed of thought; come before me quickly. Having said "Do not fear, my child, I am here" — protect me as a cow protects its calf.
contentBlocks.meanings: āvirbhava = appear, manifest | manō-vēgāt = at the speed of thought | śīghram āgachcha = come quickly | mē puraḥ = before me | mā vatsa bhai = "do not fear, dear child" | iha iti uktvā = having said thus | kāmaṃ = freely, lovingly | gauriva = like a cow | rakṣa māṃ = protect me

contentBlocks.original:
देवि शीघ्रं समागच्छ धरणीगर्भसंस्थिते ।
मातस्त्वद्भृत्यभृत्योऽहं मृगये त्वां कुतूहलात् ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvi śīghraṃ samāgachCha dharaṇīgarbhasaṃsthitē ।
mātastvadbhṛtyabhṛtyō'haṃ mṛgayē tvāṃ kutūhalāt ॥ 41 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 41:] O Goddess dwelling in the womb of the earth — come to me quickly. O Mother, I, your servant's servant, seek you with great longing.
contentBlocks.meanings: dēvi = O Goddess | śīghraṃ samāgachcha = come quickly | dharaṇī-garbha-saṃsthitē = O one dwelling in the womb of the earth | mātar = O Mother | tvat-bhṛtya-bhṛtyaḥ aham = I, the servant of your servant | mṛgayē tvāṃ = I seek you | kutūhalāt = with great longing/eagerness

contentBlocks.original:
उत्तिष्ठ जागृहि त्वं मे समुत्तिष्ठ सुजागृहि ।
अक्षयान् हेमकलशान् सुवर्णेन सुपूरितान् ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
uttiṣṭha jāgṛhi tvaṃ mē samuttiṣṭha sujāgṛhi ।
akṣayān hēmakalaśān suvarṇēna supūritān ॥ 42 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 42:] Arise! Awake for me! Arise fully, O fully awakened one — [rise with] inexhaustible golden pots filled to the brim with gold.
contentBlocks.meanings: uttiṣṭha = arise | jāgṛhi = be awake | tvaṃ mē = for me | samuttiṣṭha = arise completely | su-jāgṛhi = be fully awake | akṣayān = inexhaustible | hēma-kalaśān = golden pots | suvarṇēna = with gold | supūritān = filled to the brim

contentBlocks.original:
निक्षेपान्मे समाकृष्य समुद्धृत्य ममाग्रतः ।
समुन्नतानना भूत्वा समाधेहि धरान्तरात् ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nikṣēpānmē samākṛṣya samuddhṛtya mamāgrataḥ ।
samunnatānanā bhūtvā samādhēhi dharāntarāt ॥ 43 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 43:] Having drawn out the treasures deposited within and lifted them up, place them before me with your face raised high [in joy].
contentBlocks.meanings: nikṣēpān = the treasures/deposits | mē samākṛṣya = having drawn out for me | samuddhṛtya = having lifted up | mamāgrataḥ = before me | samunnata-ānanā bhūtvā = having become [one with] face raised high [joyfully] | samādhēhi = place firmly | dharā-antarāt = from within the earth

contentBlocks.original:
मत्सन्निधिं समागच्छ मदाहितकृपारसात् ।
प्रसीद श्रेयसां दोग्ध्री लक्ष्मीर्मे नयनाग्रतः ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
matsannidhiṃ samāgachCha madāhitakṛpārasāt ।
prasīda śrēyasāṃ dōgdhrī lakṣmīrmē nayanāgrataḥ ॥ 44 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 44:] Come to my presence from the kindness you have placed in me. O bestower of all good things, O Lakṣmī — appear before my eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: mat-sannidhiṃ samāgachcha = come to my presence | mad-āhita-kṛpā-rasāt = from the compassion-nectar you have placed within me | prasīda = be gracious | śrēyasāṃ dōgdhrī = O milker/provider of all good things | lakṣmīḥ mē nayana-agrataḥ = O Lakṣmī, [appear] before my eyes

contentBlocks.original:
अत्रोपविश लक्ष्मि त्वं स्थिरा भव हिरण्मये ।
सुस्थिरा भव सम्प्रीत्या प्रसीद वरदा भव ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
atrōpaviśa lakṣmi tvaṃ sthirā bhava hiraṇmayē ।
susthirā bhava samprītyā prasīda varadā bhava ॥ 45 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 45:] Sit here, O Lakṣmī; be stable in your golden form; be firmly established with love; O giver of boons — be gracious.
contentBlocks.meanings: atra upaviśa = sit here | lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī | sthirā bhava = be stable | hiraṇmayē = in your golden form | su-sthirā bhava = be firmly established | sam-prītyā = with love/affection | prasīda = be gracious | vara-dā bhava = be a bestower of boons

contentBlocks.original:
आनीतांस्तु तथा देवि निधीन्मे सम्प्रदर्शय ।
अद्य क्षणेन सहसा दत्त्वा संरक्ष मां सदा ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ānītāṃstu tathā dēvi nidhīnmē sampradarśaya ।
adya kṣaṇēna sahasā dattvā saṃrakṣa māṃ sadā ॥ 46 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 46:] O Goddess, show me the treasures thus brought; at this very moment, swiftly give them and protect me always.
contentBlocks.meanings: ānītān = those brought/brought forth | tu tathā dēvi = O Goddess, thus | nidhīn = treasures | mē sampradarśaya = show me fully | adya kṣaṇēna sahasā = at this very moment, suddenly | dattvā = having given | saṃrakṣa = protect | māṃ sadā = me always

contentBlocks.original:
मयि तिष्ठ तथा नित्यं यथेन्द्रादिषु तिष्ठसि ।
अभयं कुरु मे देवि महालक्ष्मीर्नमोऽस्तु ते ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mayi tiṣṭha tathā nityaṃ yathēndrādiṣu tiṣṭhasi ।
abhayaṃ kuru mē dēvi mahālakṣmīrnamō'stu tē ॥ 47 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 47:] Dwell in me always just as you dwell in Indra and other gods. Grant me fearlessness, O Goddess — O Mahālakṣmī, salutation to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: mayi tiṣṭha = dwell in me | tathā nityaṃ = thus perpetually | yathā indrādiṣu tiṣṭhasi = as you dwell in Indra and others | abhayaṃ kuru = grant fearlessness | mē dēvi = O Goddess to me | mahālakṣmīḥ namō'stu tē = salutation to you, O Mahālakṣmī

contentBlocks.original:
समागच्छ महालक्ष्मि शुद्धजाम्बूनदप्रभे ।
प्रसीद पुरतः स्थित्वा प्रणतं मां विलोकय ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samāgachCha mahālakṣmi śuddhajāmbūnadaprabhē ।
prasīda purataḥ sthitvā praṇataṃ māṃ vilōkaya ॥ 48 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 48:] Come, O Mahālakṣmī, shining like pure refined gold; be gracious; standing before me, behold me who bow to you.
contentBlocks.meanings: samāgachcha = come | mahālakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī | śuddha-jāmbūnada-prabhē = O you who shine like pure jāmbūnada gold | prasīda = be gracious | purataḥ sthitvā = having stood before me | praṇataṃ māṃ vilōkaya = behold/look upon me who bow to you

contentBlocks.original:
लक्ष्मीर्भुवं गता भासि यत्र यत्र हिरण्मयी ।
तत्र तत्र स्थिता त्वं मे तव रूपं प्रदर्शय ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
lakṣmīrbhuvaṃ gatā bhāsi yatra yatra hiraṇmayī ।
tatra tatra sthitā tvaṃ mē tava rūpaṃ pradarśaya ॥ 49 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 49:] O Lakṣmī, wherever you have gone across the earth, shining golden — wherever you have thus settled, show me your form there.
contentBlocks.meanings: lakṣmīḥ bhuvaṃ gatā bhāsi = O Lakṣmī who shines, having gone to the earth | yatra yatra hiraṇmayī = wherever [you are], the golden one | tatra tatra sthitā tvaṃ = wherever you are thus settled | mē = to me | tava rūpaṃ pradarśaya = show your form

contentBlocks.original:
क्रीडन्ती बहुधा भूमौ परिपूर्णकृपामयि ।
मम मूर्धनि ते हस्तमविलम्बितमर्पय ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
krīḍantī bahudhā bhūmau paripūrṇakṛpāmayi ।
mama mūrdhani tē hastamavilambitamarpaya ॥ 50 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 50:] O you who play in many ways on earth, full of overflowing compassion — place your hand without delay upon my head.
contentBlocks.meanings: krīḍantī = playing | bahudhā = in many ways | bhūmau = on earth | paripūrṇa-kṛpā-mayi = full of overflowing compassion | mama mūrdhani = on my head | tē hastam = your hand | avilambitam = without delay, immediately | arpaya = place/offer

contentBlocks.original:
फलद्भाग्योदये लक्ष्मि समस्तपुरवासिनी ।
प्रसीद मे महालक्ष्मि परिपूर्णमनोरथे ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
phaladbhāgyōdayē lakṣmi samastapuravāsinī ।
prasīda mē mahālakṣmi paripūrṇamanōrathē ॥ 51 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 51:] O Lakṣmī whose fortune is ripening to fruition, who dwells in all cities — be gracious to me, O Mahālakṣmī; fulfil all my heart's desires.
contentBlocks.meanings: phalad-bhāgya-udayē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī whose fortune is rising and bearing fruit | samasta-pura-vāsinī = O dweller in all cities | prasīda = be gracious | mē mahālakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī to me | paripūrṇa-manōrathē = O fulfiller of all heart's desires

contentBlocks.original:
अयोध्यादिषु सर्वेषु नगरेषु समास्थिते ।
वैभवैर्विविधैर्युक्तैः समागच्छ मुदान्विते ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ayōdhyādiṣu sarvēṣu nagarēṣu samāsthitē ।
vaibhavairvividhairyuktaiḥ samāgachCha mudānvitē ॥ 52 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 52:] In Ayōdhyā and all other cities — adorned with various abundant splendours — come with joy, O Lakṣmī.
contentBlocks.meanings: ayōdhyā-ādiṣu sarvēṣu = in Ayōdhyā and all other [cities] | nagarēṣu = in cities | samāsthitē = O one who is established/dwelling | vaibhavaiḥ vividhaiḥ yuktaiḥ = adorned/equipped with various splendours | samāgachcha = come | mudā anvitē = O one endowed with joy

contentBlocks.original:
समागच्छ समागच्छ ममाग्रे भव सुस्थिरा ।
करुणारसनिष्यन्दनेत्रद्वय विलासिनी ॥ 53 ॥ [निष्पन्न]
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samāgachCha samāgachCha mamāgrē bhava susthirā ।
karuṇārasaniṣyandanētradvaya vilāsinī ॥ 53 ॥ [niṣpanna]
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 53:] Come, come again and again; stand firmly before me; O you who play with eyes overflowing with nectar-compassion — be near me.
contentBlocks.meanings: samāgachcha samāgachcha = come, come | mamāgrē = before me | bhava su-sthirā = be firmly stable | karuṇā-rasa-niṣyanda-nētra-dvaya-vilāsinī = O one who plays with eyes overflowing with nectar-compassion

contentBlocks.original:
सन्निधत्स्व महालक्ष्मि त्वत्पाणिं मम मस्तके ।
करुणासुधया मां त्वमभिषिञ्च्य स्थिरं कुरु ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sannidhatsva mahālakṣmi tvatpāṇiṃ mama mastakē ।
karuṇāsudhayā māṃ tvamabhiṣiñchya sthiraṃ kuru ॥ 54 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 54:] Be near me, O Mahālakṣmī; place your hand upon my head; having consecrated me with the nectar of your compassion, make me steadfast.
contentBlocks.meanings: sannidhatsva = be near | mahālakṣmi = O Mahālakṣmī | tvat-pāṇiṃ = your hand | mama mastakē = upon my head | karuṇā-sudhayā = with the nectar of compassion | māṃ tvam abhiṣiñchya = having consecrated/sprinkled me | sthiraṃ kuru = make steadfast

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वराजगृहे लक्ष्मि समागच्छ बलान्विते । [मुदान्विते]
स्थित्वाऽऽशु पुरतो मेऽद्य प्रसादेनाऽभयं कुरु ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvarājagṛhē lakṣmi samāgachCha balānvitē । [mudānvitē]
sthitvā''śu puratō mē'dya prasādēnā'bhayaṃ kuru ॥ 55 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 55:] O Lakṣmī dwelling in the houses of all kings — come with your full power; standing quickly before me today, grant me fearlessness by your grace.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-rāja-gṛhē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī in the houses of all kings | samāgachcha = come | balānvitē = endowed with full power | sthitvā āśu puratō mē = standing quickly before me | adya = today | prasādēna = by your grace | abhayaṃ kuru = grant fearlessness

contentBlocks.original:
सादरं मस्तके हस्तं मम त्वं कृपयार्पय ।
सर्वराजगृहे लक्ष्मि त्वत्कला मयि तिष्ठतु ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sādaraṃ mastakē hastaṃ mama tvaṃ kṛpayārpaya ।
sarvarājagṛhē lakṣmi tvatkalā mayi tiṣṭhatu ॥ 56 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 56:] Lovingly place your hand on my head; O Lakṣmī in all royal abodes — may your divine portion (kalā) abide in me.
contentBlocks.meanings: sādaraṃ = lovingly, with care | mastakē hastaṃ = hand on head | mama tvaṃ = yours upon mine | kṛpayā arpaya = offer with compassion | sarva-rāja-gṛhē lakṣmi = O Lakṣmī in all royal homes | tvat-kalā mayi tiṣṭhatu = may your divine portion abide in me

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादि श्रीमहालक्ष्मि विष्णुवामाङ्कसंस्थिते ।
प्रत्यक्षं कुरु मे रूपं रक्ष मां शरणागतम् ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādi śrīmahālakṣmi viṣṇuvāmāṅkasaṃsthitē ।
pratyakṣaṃ kuru mē rūpaṃ rakṣa māṃ śaraṇāgatam ॥ 57 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 57:] O Ādi Śrī Mahālakṣmī dwelling at Viṣṇu's left side — make your form visible to me; protect me who have taken refuge in you.
contentBlocks.meanings: ādyādi śrī-mahālakṣmi = O Ādi Śrī Mahālakṣmī | viṣṇu-vāma-aṅka-saṃsthitē = O one seated at Viṣṇu's left side | pratyakṣaṃ kuru mē rūpaṃ = make your form directly visible to me | rakṣa māṃ śaraṇāgatam = protect me who have taken refuge

contentBlocks.original:
प्रसीद मे महालक्ष्मि सुप्रसीद महाशिवे ।
अचला भव सम्प्रीत्या सुस्थिरा भव मद्गृहे ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
prasīda mē mahālakṣmi suprasīda mahāśivē ।
achalā bhava samprītyā susthirā bhava madgṛhē ॥ 58 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 58:] Be gracious to me, O Mahālakṣmī; be supremely gracious, O great Śivā; become immovable with love; become firmly stable in my home.
contentBlocks.meanings: prasīda mē mahālakṣmi = be gracious to me, O Mahālakṣmī | su-prasīda = be supremely gracious | mahā-śivē = O great auspicious one | achalā bhava = become immovable | sam-prītyā = with love | su-sthirā bhava = become firmly stable | mad-gṛhē = in my home

contentBlocks.original:
यावत्तिष्ठन्ति वेदाश्च यावच्चन्द्रदिवाकरौ ।
यावद्विष्णुश्च यावत्त्वं तावत्कुरु कृपां मयि ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yāvattiṣṭhanti vēdāścha yāvachchandradivākarau ।
yāvadviṣṇuścha yāvattvaṃ tāvatkuru kṛpāṃ mayi ॥ 59 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 59:] For as long as the Vedas exist, as long as the sun and moon, as long as Viṣṇu exists, and as long as you yourself exist — show compassion to me for all that time.
contentBlocks.meanings: yāvat tiṣṭhanti vēdāḥ = as long as the Vedas exist | yāvat chandra-divākarau = as long as moon and sun | yāvat viṣṇuḥ = as long as Viṣṇu | yāvat tvaṃ = as long as you | tāvat kuru kṛpāṃ mayi = for all that time, show compassion to me

contentBlocks.original:
चान्द्रीकला यथा शुक्ले वर्धते सा दिने दिने ।
तथा दया ते मय्येव वर्धतामभिवर्धताम् ॥ 60 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
chāndrīkalā yathā śuklē vardhatē sā dinē dinē ।
tathā dayā tē mayyēva vardhatāmabhivardhatām ॥ 60 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 60:] Just as the moon's disc waxes day by day in the bright fortnight, may your compassion towards me likewise wax and grow and grow ever more.
contentBlocks.meanings: chāndrī-kalā yathā = just as the moon's disc | śuklē = in the bright/waxing fortnight | vardhatē = waxes/grows | sā dinē dinē = day by day | tathā dayā tē mayyēva = likewise your compassion in me | vardhatāṃ abhivardhatām = may it grow, may it grow even more (double intensification)

contentBlocks.original:
यथा वैकुण्ठनगरे यथा वै क्षीरसागरे ।
तथा मद्भवने तिष्ठ स्थिरं श्रीविष्णुना सह ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā vaikuṇṭhanagarē yathā vai kṣīrasāgarē ।
tathā madbhavanē tiṣṭha sthiraṃ śrīviṣṇunā saha ॥ 61 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 61:] Just as you dwell in the city of Vaikuṇṭha and in the Milk-Ocean — dwell steadily in my home too, together with Śrī Viṣṇu.
contentBlocks.meanings: yathā vaikuṇṭha-nagarē = just as in the city of Vaikuṇṭha | yathā vai kṣīra-sāgarē = and just as in the milk-ocean | tathā mad-bhavanē = likewise in my home | tiṣṭha = dwell | sthiraṃ = steadily | śrī-viṣṇunā saha = together with Śrī Viṣṇu

contentBlocks.original:
योगिनां हृदये नित्यं यथा तिष्ठसि विष्णुना ।
तथा मद्भवने तिष्ठ स्थिरं श्रीविष्णुना सह ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yōgināṃ hṛdayē nityaṃ yathā tiṣṭhasi viṣṇunā ।
tathā madbhavanē tiṣṭha sthiraṃ śrīviṣṇunā saha ॥ 62 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 62:] Just as you dwell eternally in yogīs' hearts with Viṣṇu — dwell steadily in my home too, together with Śrī Viṣṇu.
contentBlocks.meanings: yōgināṃ hṛdayē nityaṃ = eternally in the hearts of yogīs | yathā tiṣṭhasi = as you dwell | viṣṇunā = with Viṣṇu | tathā mad-bhavanē tiṣṭha = likewise dwell in my home | sthiraṃ = steadily | śrī-viṣṇunā saha = together with Śrī Viṣṇu

contentBlocks.original:
नारायणस्य हृदये भवती यथास्ते
नारायणोऽपि तव हृत्कमले यथास्ते ।
नारायणस्त्वमपि नित्यमुभौ तथैव
तौ तिष्ठतां हृदि ममापि दयान्वितौ श्रीः ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
nārāyaṇasya hṛdayē bhavatī yathāstē
nārāyaṇō'pi tava hṛtkamalē yathāstē ।
nārāyaṇastvamapi nityamubhau tathaiva
tau tiṣṭhatāṃ hṛdi mamāpi dayānvitau śrīḥ ॥ 63 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 63:] Just as you dwell in Nārāyaṇa's heart and Nārāyaṇa dwells in your lotus-heart, just as you and Nārāyaṇa are thus eternally and equally [united] — may both of you dwell with compassion in my heart also, O Śrī.
contentBlocks.meanings: nārāyaṇasya hṛdayē bhavatī yathā astē = as you dwell in Nārāyaṇa's heart | nārāyaṇaḥ api tava hṛt-kamalē yathā astē = as Nārāyaṇa also dwells in your lotus-heart | nārāyaṇaḥ tvam api = you and Nārāyaṇa | nityam ubhau = both eternally | tau tiṣṭhatāṃ hṛdi mama api = may both dwell in my heart too | dayā-anvitau = with compassion | śrīḥ = O Śrī

contentBlocks.original:
विज्ञानवृद्धिं हृदये कुरु श्रीः
सौभाग्यवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः ।
दयासुवृद्धिं कुरुतां मयि श्रीः
सुवर्णवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vijñānavṛddhiṃ hṛdayē kuru śrīḥ
saubhāgyavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ ।
dayāsuvṛddhiṃ kurutāṃ mayi śrīḥ
suvarṇavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ ॥ 64 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 64:] O Śrī — increase wisdom in my heart! O Śrī — increase auspiciousness in my home! O Śrī — let compassion increase in me! O Śrī — increase gold in my home!
contentBlocks.meanings: vijñāna-vṛddhiṃ hṛdayē kuru = increase wisdom in the heart | śrīḥ = O Śrī | saubhāgya-vṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē = increase auspiciousness in my home | dayā-su-vṛddhiṃ kurutāṃ mayi = let compassion greatly increase in me | suvarṇa-vṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē = increase gold in my home

contentBlocks.original:
न मां त्यजेथाः श्रितकल्पवल्लि
सद्भक्तचिन्तामणिकामधेनो ।
विश्वस्य मातर्भव सुप्रसन्ना
गृहे कलत्रेषु च पुत्रवर्गे ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
na māṃ tyajēthāḥ śritakalpavalli
sadbhaktachintāmaṇikāmadhēnō ।
viśvasya mātarbhava suprasannā
gṛhē kalatrēṣu cha putravargē ॥ 65 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 65:] Do not abandon me, O divine vine/wish-fulfilling tree, O wish-gem among devotees, O wish-cow! Be gracious to me, O Mother of all worlds — in my family, my spouse, and my children.
contentBlocks.meanings: na māṃ tyajēthāḥ = do not abandon me | śrita-kalpa-valli = O divine vine (kalpa-valli = wish-fulfilling creeper) | sat-bhakta-chintāmaṇi = O wish-gem (chintāmaṇi) of true devotees | kāma-dhēnō = O wish-cow | viśvasya mātar = O Mother of the world | bhava su-prasannā = be gracious | gṛhē = in the home | kalatēṣu = among spouses | cha putra-vargē = and children

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिमाये त्वमजाण्डबीजं
त्वमेव साकारनिराकृतिस्त्वम् ।
त्वया धृताश्चाब्जभवाण्डसङ्घा-
-श्चित्रं चरित्रं तव देवि विष्णोः ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādimāyē tvamajāṇḍabījaṃ
tvamēva sākāranirākṛtistvam ।
tvayā dhṛtāśchābjabhavāṇḍasaṅghā-
-śchitraṃ charitraṃ tava dēvi viṣṇōḥ ॥ 66 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 66:] O Ādi Māyā — you are the seed of the unmanifest egg of creation; you are both with form and without form; held by you are all the universe-eggs born from Brahmā; wonderful is your divine play, O Goddess — and that of Viṣṇu.
contentBlocks.meanings: ādyādi-māyē = O Ādi Māyā (primordial creative power) | tvam aja-aṇḍa-bījaṃ = you are the seed of the unmanifest cosmic egg | tvam ēva sākāra-nirākṛtiḥ = you are both with form and without form | tvayā dhṛtāḥ = held by you | cha abja-bhava-aṇḍa-saṅghāḥ = the groups of universe-eggs born from Brahmā's lotus | chitraṃ charitram = wonderful is the nature/play | tava dēvi viṣṇōḥ = yours O Goddess and of Viṣṇu

contentBlocks.original:
ब्रह्मरुद्रादयो देवा वेदाश्चापि न शक्नुयुः ।
महिमानं तव स्तोतुं मन्दोऽहं शक्नुयां कथम् ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
brahmarudrādayō dēvā vēdāśchāpi na śaknuyuḥ ।
mahimānaṃ tava stōtuṃ mandō'haṃ śaknuyāṃ katham ॥ 67 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 67:] Even Brahmā, Rudra, and the other gods — even the Vedas themselves — are not capable of praising your greatness. How then can this dull-witted one [like me] be capable?
contentBlocks.meanings: brahma-rudra-ādayaḥ dēvāḥ = Brahmā, Rudra, and the other gods | vēdāḥ cha api = and even the Vedas | na śaknuyuḥ = are not capable | mahimānaṃ tava stōtuṃ = of praising your greatness | mandaḥ ahaṃ = this dull-witted one, I | śaknuyāṃ katham = how can I be capable?

contentBlocks.original:
अम्ब त्वद्वत्सवाक्यानि सूक्तासूक्तानि यानि च ।
तानि स्वीकुरु सर्वज्ञे दयालुत्वेन सादरम् ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
amba tvadvatsavākyāni sūktāsūktāni yāni cha ।
tāni svīkuru sarvajñē dayālutvēna sādaram ॥ 68 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 68:] O Mother, whatever words I have spoken to you — whether appropriate or inappropriate — accept all of them with kindness, O all-knowing one.
contentBlocks.meanings: amba = O Mother | tvad-vatsa-vākyāni = the words of your child [to you] | sūktāsūktāni = well-said and not-well-said | yāni cha = and whatever | tāni svīkuru = accept all of them | sarvajñē = O all-knowing one | dayālutvēna = with compassion | sādaram = with care/respect

contentBlocks.original:
भवतीं शरणं गत्वा कृतार्थाः स्युः पुरातनाः ।
इति सञ्चिन्त्य मनसा त्वामहं शरणं व्रजे ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bhavatīṃ śaraṇaṃ gatvā kṛtārthāḥ syuḥ purātanāḥ ।
iti sañchintya manasā tvāmahaṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajē ॥ 69 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 69:] Thinking thus — that ancient ones who took refuge in you were fulfilled — with that thought in my mind, I take refuge in you.
contentBlocks.meanings: bhavatīṃ śaraṇaṃ gatvā = having taken refuge in you | kṛtārthāḥ syuḥ = they were fulfilled | purātanāḥ = the ancients (former devotees) | iti sañchintya manasā = thinking thus in my mind | tvām ahaṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajē = I take refuge in you

contentBlocks.original:
अनन्ता नित्यसुखिनस्त्वद्भक्तास्त्वत्परायणाः ।
इति वेदप्रमाणाद्धि देवि त्वां शरणं व्रजे ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anantā nityasukhinastvadbhaktāstvatparāyaṇāḥ ।
iti vēdapramāṇāddhi dēvi tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajē ॥ 70 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 70:] By Vedic authority, your devotees — who are devoted to you as the highest — are endless and eternally happy. O Goddess, with this certainty, I take refuge in you.
contentBlocks.meanings: anantāḥ = endless, innumerable | nitya-sukhinaḥ = eternally happy | tvad-bhaktāḥ = your devotees | tvat-parāyaṇāḥ = devoted to you as the highest | iti vēda-pramāṇāt = by the authority/proof of the Vedas | hi = indeed | dēvi = O Goddess | tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ vrajē = I take refuge in you

contentBlocks.original:
तव प्रतिज्ञा मद्भक्ता न नश्यन्तीत्यपि क्वचित् ।
इति सञ्चिन्त्य सञ्चिन्त्य प्राणान् सन्धारयाम्यहम् ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tava pratijñā madbhaktā na naśyantītyapi kvachit ।
iti sañchintya sañchintya prāṇān sandhārayāmyaham ॥ 71 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 71:] Your pledge — that "my devotees shall never perish anywhere" — thinking and thinking upon this again and again, I sustain my life.
contentBlocks.meanings: tava pratijñā = your pledge/promise | mat-bhaktāḥ = my devotees | na naśyanti = shall not perish | iti api kvachit = not anywhere, never | iti sañchintya sañchintya = thinking and thinking (repeated deliberation) | prāṇān sandhārayāmi aham = I sustain my life/breath

contentBlocks.original:
त्वदधीनस्त्वहं मातस्त्वत्कृपा मयि विद्यते ।
यावत्सम्पूर्णकामः स्यात्तावद्देहि दयानिधे ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
tvadadhīnastvahaṃ mātastvatkṛpā mayi vidyatē ।
yāvatsampūrṇakāmaḥ syāttāvaddēhi dayānidhē ॥ 72 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 72:] I am under your authority, O Mother; your grace rests upon me. For as long as [I am] completely fulfilled [by your grace], that long grant [me your favour], O treasure-house of compassion.
contentBlocks.meanings: tvad-adhīnaḥ = under your authority | tvam aham = I am yours | mātar = O Mother | tvat-kṛpā mayi vidyatē = your grace exists/rests in me | yāvat sampūrṇa-kāmaḥ syāt = for as long as [I may be] fully fulfilled | tāvat dēhi = that long, give | dayā-nidhē = O treasure-house of compassion

contentBlocks.original:
क्षणमात्रं न शक्नोमि जीवितुं त्वत्कृपां विना ।
न जीवन्तीह जलजा जलं त्यक्त्वा जलग्रहाः ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṣaṇamātraṃ na śaknōmi jīvituṃ tvatkṛpāṃ vinā ।
na jīvantīha jalajā jalaṃ tyaktvā jalagrahāḥ ॥ 73 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 73:] For even a moment I cannot live without your grace — just as aquatic creatures that live in water cannot survive when water is left behind.
contentBlocks.meanings: kṣaṇa-mātraṃ = for even a moment | na śaknōmi jīvituṃ = I cannot live | tvat-kṛpāṃ vinā = without your grace | na jīvanti = do not live | iha = here | jalajā = aquatic creatures (born in water) | jalaṃ tyaktvā = having left water | jala-grahāḥ = water-seizing creatures (those that live in water)

contentBlocks.original:
यथा हि पुत्रवात्सल्याज्जननी प्रस्नुतस्तनी ।
वत्सं त्वरितमागत्य सम्प्रीणयति वत्सला ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā hi putravātsalyājjananī prasnutastanī ।
vatsaṃ tvaritamāgatya samprīṇayati vatsalā ॥ 74 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 74:] Just as a mother, overflowing with love for her child and with milk-swollen breasts, rushes quickly to her calf and lovingly nourishes it —
contentBlocks.meanings: yathā hi = just as | putra-vātsalyāt = from love for her child | jananī = a mother | prasnuta-stanī = with milk-dripping breasts (a cow or nursing mother) | vatsaṃ = to the calf/child | tvaritam āgatya = having come quickly | samprīṇayati = nourishes lovingly | vatsalā = the affectionate one

contentBlocks.original:
यदि स्यां तव पुत्रोऽहं माता त्वं यदि मामकी ।
दयापयोधरस्तन्यसुधाभिरभिषिञ्च माम् ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yadi syāṃ tava putrō'haṃ mātā tvaṃ yadi māmakī ।
dayāpayōdharastanyasudhābhirabhiṣiñcha mām ॥ 75 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 75:] [Completing the simile:] If I am your son and you are my mother — sprinkle me with the nectar of your compassion-filled breasts.
contentBlocks.meanings: yadi syāṃ tava putraḥ aham = if I am your son | mātā tvaṃ yadi māmakī = if you are my mother | dayā-payōdharaḥ = breasts filled with compassion | stanya-sudhābhiḥ = with the nectar of [your] milk | abhiṣiñcha māṃ = sprinkle/consecrate me

contentBlocks.original:
मृग्यो न गुणलेशोऽपि मयि दोषैकमन्दिरे ।
पांसूनां वृष्टिबिन्दूनां दोषाणां च न मे मतिः ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mṛgyō na guṇalēśō'pi mayi dōṣaikamandirē ।
pāṃsūnāṃ vṛṣṭibindūnāṃ dōṣāṇāṃ cha na mē matiḥ ॥ 76 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 76:] Not even a trace of virtue is to be found in me — I am the sole home of faults. I have no sense of how many grains of dust, drops of rain, or sins I have accumulated.
contentBlocks.meanings: mṛgyaḥ na guṇa-lēśaḥ api = not even a trace of virtue is to be found (mṛgya = to be searched for) | mayi = in me | dōṣa-ēka-mandirē = [I am] the sole abode of faults | pāṃsūnāṃ vṛṣṭi-bindūnāṃ = of grains of dust and drops of rain | dōṣāṇāṃ cha = and of sins | na mē matiḥ = I have no count/awareness

contentBlocks.original:
पापिनामहमेवाग्र्यो दयालूनां त्वमग्रणीः ।
दयनीयो मदन्योऽस्ति तव कोऽत्र जगत्त्रये ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pāpināmahamēvāgryō dayālūnāṃ tvamagraṇīḥ ।
dayanīyō madanyō'sti tava kō'tra jagattrayē ॥ 77 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 77:] I am the foremost among sinners; you are the foremost among the compassionate. Who else in all three worlds is more worthy of your compassion than me?
contentBlocks.meanings: pāpināṃ aham ēva agryaḥ = I am the very foremost among sinners | dayālūnāṃ tvaṃ agraṇīḥ = you are the chief/foremost among the compassionate | dayanīyaḥ = one worthy of compassion | mad-anyaḥ = other than me | asti = exists | tava kaḥ = for you, who | atra jagat-trayē = here in the three worlds

contentBlocks.original:
विधिनाहं न सृष्टश्चेन्न स्यात्तव दयालुता ।
आमयो वा न सृष्टश्चेदौषधस्य वृथोदयः ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vidhināhaṃ na sṛṣṭaśchēnna syāttava dayālutā ।
āmayō vā na sṛṣṭaśchēdauṣadhasya vṛthōdayaḥ ॥ 78 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 78:] If I had not been created by Brahmā, your compassion would have no proper object; just as if disease had not been created, the physician's medicine would arise in vain.
contentBlocks.meanings: vidhina = by Brahmā (the ordainer) | ahaṃ na sṛṣṭaḥ chet = if I had not been created | na syāt tava dayālatā = your compassion would not exist (would have no purpose) | āmayaḥ vā na sṛṣṭaḥ chet = if disease had not been created | auṣadhasya vṛthā udayaḥ = the arising of medicine would be in vain

contentBlocks.original:
कृपा मदग्रजा किं ते अहं किं वा तदग्रजः ।
विचार्य देहि मे वित्तं तव देवि दयानिधे ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kṛpā madagrajā kiṃ tē ahaṃ kiṃ vā tadagrajaḥ ।
vichārya dēhi mē vittaṃ tava dēvi dayānidhē ॥ 79 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 79:] Which was born first — your compassion or I? Which is the elder of the two — think this over, and give me wealth, O Goddess, O treasure-house of compassion.
contentBlocks.meanings: kṛpā = compassion | mat-agrajā = elder/firstborn before me | kiṃ tē = is [it] yours | ahaṃ kiṃ vā tad-agra-jaḥ = or am I the elder before that [compassion] | vichārya = having thought over/deliberated | dēhi mē vittaṃ = give me wealth | tava dēvi dayā-nidhē = O Goddess, O treasure-house of compassion

contentBlocks.original:
माता पिता त्वं गुरुसद्गतिः श्री-
-स्त्वमेव सञ्जीवनहेतुभूता ।
अन्यं न मन्ये जगदेकनाथे
त्वमेव सर्वं मम देवि सत्ये ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mātā pitā tvaṃ gurusadgatiḥ śrī-
-stvamēva sañjīvanahētubhūtā ।
anyaṃ na manyē jagadēkanāthē
tvamēva sarvaṃ mama dēvi satyē ॥ 80 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 80:] You are my mother, my father, my teacher, my good path (Śrī); you alone are the cause of my life's restoration. O sole Lord of all worlds — I acknowledge no other; you alone are everything to me, O Goddess, in truth.
contentBlocks.meanings: mātā = mother | pitā = father | tvaṃ = you | guru-sad-gatiḥ śrīḥ = the Śrī who is teacher and good path | tvam ēva sañjīvana-hētu-bhūtā = you alone are the cause of reviving/sustaining life | anyaṃ na manyē = I acknowledge/respect no other | jagad-ēka-nāthē = O sole Lord of all worlds | tvam ēva sarvaṃ = you alone are everything | mama dēvi satyē = to me, O Goddess, in truth

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिलक्ष्मीर्भव सुप्रसन्ना
विशुद्धविज्ञानसुखैकदोग्ध्री ।
अज्ञानहन्त्री त्रिगुणातिरिक्ता
प्रज्ञाननेत्री भव सुप्रसन्ना ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādilakṣmīrbhava suprasannā
viśuddhavijñānasukhaikadōgdhrī ।
ajñānahantrī triguṇātiriktā
prajñānanētrī bhava suprasannā ॥ 81 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 81:] O Ādi-Lakṣmī — be supremely gracious; you are the sole giver of the happiness of pure spiritual knowledge; you destroy ignorance; you transcend the three guṇas; you are the guide of wisdom — be supremely gracious.
contentBlocks.meanings: ādyādi-lakṣmīḥ = O Ādi-Lakṣmī | bhava su-prasannā = be supremely gracious | viśuddha-vijñāna-sukha-ēka-dōgdhrī = the sole milk-giver of the happiness of pure spiritual wisdom | ajñāna-hantrī = destroyer of ignorance | tri-guṇa-atiriktā = transcending the three guṇas | prajñāna-nētrī = guide of [higher] wisdom | bhava su-prasannā = be supremely gracious

contentBlocks.original:
अशेषवाग्जाड्यमलापहन्त्री
नवं नवं स्पष्टसुवाक्प्रदायिनी ।
ममेह जिह्वाग्र सुरङ्गनर्तकी [नर्तिनी]
भव प्रसन्ना वदने च मे श्रीः ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aśēṣavāgjāḍyamalāpahantrī
navaṃ navaṃ spaṣṭasuvākpradāyinī ।
mamēha jihvāgra suraṅganartakī [nartinī]
bhava prasannā vadanē cha mē śrīḥ ॥ 82 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 82:] O you who remove all the impurity of speechlessness and dullness, O bestower of fresh, clear, new speech — dance on the tip of my tongue like a divine dancer; O Śrī, be gracious in my face.
contentBlocks.meanings: aśēṣa-vāg-jāḍya-mala-apa-hantrī = remover of all impurity/dullness of speech | navaṃ navaṃ spaṣṭa-su-vāk-pradāyinī = bestower of ever-new, clear, beautiful speech | mama iha jihvā-agra = on the tip of my tongue here | surāṅga-nartakī = divine dancer (nartakī = female dancer; surāṅganā = celestial maiden) | bhava prasannā = be gracious | vadanē cha mē śrīḥ = in my face, O Śrī

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तसम्पत्सुविराजमाना
समस्ततेजश्चयभासमाना ।
विष्णुप्रिये त्वं भव दीप्यमाना
वाग्देवता मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastasampatsuvirājamānā
samastatējaśchayabhāsamānā ।
viṣṇupriyē tvaṃ bhava dīpyamānā
vāgdēvatā mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 83 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 83:] Shining with all wealth, blazing with the accumulation of all radiance — O beloved of Viṣṇu, may you glow brilliantly; O speech-goddess, may you be gracious in my eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: samasta-sampat-su-virājamānā = shining beautifully with all wealth | samasta-tējaḥ-chaya-bhāsamānā = blazing with the mass of all radiance | viṣṇu-priyē tvaṃ = O beloved of Viṣṇu, you | bhava dīpyamānā = may you glow/blaze | vāg-dēvatā mē nayanē prasannā = O speech-goddess, be gracious in my eyes

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वप्रदर्शे सकलार्थदे त्वं
प्रभासुलावण्यदयाप्रदोग्ध्री ।
सुवर्णदे त्वं सुमुखी भव श्री-
-र्हिरण्मयी मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvapradarśē sakalārthadē tvaṃ
prabhāsulāvaṇyadayāpradōgdhrī ।
suvarṇadē tvaṃ sumukhī bhava śrī-
-rhiraṇmayī mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 84 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 84:] O revealer of all things, O giver of all purposes, O bestower of radiant beauty, grace, and compassion, O golden one — be gracious in my eyes, O Śrī.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarva-pradarśē = O revealer of all | sakala-artha-dē = O giver of all purposes/meanings | prabhā-su-lāvaṇya-dayā-pra-dōgdhrī = bestower of radiance, beauty, and compassion | suvarṇa-dē = O giver of gold | tvaṃ su-mukhī bhava = be gracious (lit. be of beautiful face) | śrīḥ hiraṇmayī = O Śrī, O golden one | mē nayanē prasannā = be gracious in my eyes

contentBlocks.original:
सर्वार्थदा सर्वजगत्प्रसूतिः
सर्वेश्वरी सर्वभयापहन्त्री ।
सर्वोन्नता त्वं सुमुखी भव श्री-
-र्हिरण्मयी मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
sarvārthadā sarvajagatprasūtiḥ
sarvēśvarī sarvabhayāpahantrī ।
sarvōnnatā tvaṃ sumukhī bhava śrī-
-rhiraṇmayī mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 85 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 85:] O giver of all purposes, O mother of all worlds, O destroyer of all fear, O supremely exalted one — be gracious in my eyes, O Śrī, O golden one.
contentBlocks.meanings: sarvārthadā = O giver of all purposes | sarva-jagat-prasūtiḥ = O mother/origin of all the world | sarvēśvarī = O ruler of all | sarva-bhaya-apa-hantrī = O destroyer of all fears | sarva-unnatā = O supremely exalted one | tvaṃ su-mukhī bhava = be gracious | śrīḥ hiraṇmayī = O Śrī, O golden one | mē nayanē prasannā = be gracious in my eyes

contentBlocks.original:
समस्तविघ्नौघविनाशकारिणी
समस्तभक्तोद्धरणे विचक्षणा ।
अनन्तसौभाग्यसुखप्रदायिनी
हिरण्मयी मे नयने प्रसन्ना ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
samastavighnaughavināśakāriṇī
samastabhaktōddharaṇē vichakṣaṇā ।
anantasaubhāgyasukhapradāyinī
hiraṇmayī mē nayanē prasannā ॥ 86 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 86:] O destroyer of all hordes of obstacles, O she who is skilled in uplifting all devotees, O bestower of infinite fortune and happiness — O golden one, be gracious in my eyes.
contentBlocks.meanings: samasta-vighna-ogha-vināśa-kāriṇī = O she who destroys all hordes of obstacles | samasta-bhakta-uddharaṇē vichakṣaṇā = skilled/expert in uplifting/liberating all devotees | ananta-saubhāgya-sukha-pradāyinī = bestower of infinite fortune and happiness | hiraṇmayī = O golden one | mē nayanē prasannā = be gracious in my eyes

contentBlocks.original:
देवि प्रसीद दयनीयतमाय मह्यं
देवाधिनाथभवदेवगणाभिवन्द्ये ।
मातस्तथैव भव सन्निहिता दृशोर्मे
पत्या समं मम मुखे भव सुप्रसन्ना ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēvi prasīda dayanīyatamāya mahyaṃ
dēvādhināthabhavadēvagaṇābhivandyē ।
mātastathaiva bhava sannihitā dṛśōrmē
patyā samaṃ mama mukhē bhava suprasannā ॥ 87 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 87:] O Goddess — be gracious to me, the most pitiable of all; O you who are honoured by Brahmā and all the divine hosts; O Mother, likewise be present before my eyes, together with your Lord.
contentBlocks.meanings: dēvi prasīda = O Goddess, be gracious | dayanīya-tamāya mahyaṃ = to me who am the most worthy of pity | dēva-adhi-nātha-bhavad-ēva-gaṇa-abhivandyē = O you who are honoured by Brahmā-the-lord-of-gods and all the divine hosts | mātar = O Mother | tathā ēva = likewise | bhava sannihitā = be present/nearby | dṛśoḥ mē = before my two eyes | patyā samam = together with your Lord | mama mukhē = in my face | bhava su-prasannā = be supremely gracious

contentBlocks.original:
मा वत्स भैरभयदानकरोऽर्पितस्ते
मौलौ ममेति मयि दीनदयानुकम्पे ।
मातः समर्पय मुदा करुणाकटाक्षं
माङ्गल्यबीजमिह नः सृज जन्म मातः ॥ 88 ॥
