contentBlocks.transliteration:
mā vatsa bhairabhayadānakarō'rpitastē
maulau mamēti mayi dīnadayānukampē ।
mātaḥ samarpaya mudā karuṇākaṭākṣaṃ
māṅgaḻyabījamiha naḥ sṛja janma mātaḥ ॥ 88 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 88:] O compassionate one who responds to the humble — "Do not fear, dear child; the hand of fearlessness is placed upon your head — this is mine [my pledge]" — O Mother, thus offering your compassionate grace-glance joyfully, create the seed of all auspiciousness in us; create our birth-day.
contentBlocks.meanings: mā vatsa bhai = do not fear, dear child | abhaya-dāna-karaḥ = the hand granting fearlessness | arpitaḥ tē maulau = placed upon your head | mama iti = "this is mine [my pledge]" | mayi dīna-dayā-anukampē = O you who show compassion to the humble | mātar samarpaya mudā = O Mother, offer joyfully | karuṇā-kaṭākṣaṃ = the compassionate grace-glance | māṅgalya-bījam iha naḥ sṛja = create the seed of auspiciousness in us | janma mātaḥ = birth/life, O Mother

contentBlocks.original:
कटाक्ष इह कामधुक्तव मनस्तु चिन्तामणिः
करः सुरतरुः सदा नवनिधिस्त्वमेवेन्दिरे ।
भवे तव दयारसो मम रसायनं चान्वहं
मुखं तव कलानिधिर्विविधवाञ्छितार्थप्रदम् ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kaṭākṣa iha kāmadhuktava manastu chintāmaṇiḥ
karaḥ surataruḥ sadā navanidhistvamēvēndirē ।
bhavē tava dayārasō mama rasāyanaṃ chānvahaṃ
mukhaṃ tava kalānidhirvividhavāñChitārthapradam ॥ 89 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 89:] O Indirā — your grace-glance here is the wish-fulfilling cow; your mind is the wish-gem; your hand is the wish-tree; you yourself are the nine treasures. May the nectar of your grace be my daily elixir. May your face be the moon-lamp that illumines my days.
contentBlocks.meanings: kaṭākṣa = grace-glance | iha = here | kāma-dhuk = wish-fulfilling cow | tava = yours | manaḥ = mind | tu = but | chintāmaṇiḥ = wish-gem | karaḥ = hand | sura-taruḥ = divine/wish-tree | sadā = always | nava-nidhiḥ = nine treasures (the nine divine treasures of Kubera) | tvam ēva = you yourself | indiṝ = O Indirā (Lakṣmī) | bhavē tava dayā-rasaḥ = may the nectar of your grace | mama rasāyanaṃ = be my rasāyana (life-elixir) | anvahan = daily | mukhaṃ tava = your face | kala-nidhi = moon (treasure of rays) | aharniśam = day and night

contentBlocks.original:
यथा रसस्पर्शनतोऽयसोऽपि
सुवर्णता स्यात्कमले तथा ते ।
कटाक्षसंस्पर्शनतो जनाना-
-ममङ्गलानामपि मङ्गलत्वम् ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yathā rasasparśanatō'yasō'pi
suvarṇatā syātkamalē tathā tē ।
kaṭākṣasaṃsparśanatō janānā-
-mamaṅgaḻānāmapi maṅgaḻatvam ॥ 90 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 90:] Just as iron becomes gold by contact with the philosopher's stone, O Kamalā — similarly, even the inauspicious become auspicious by contact with your grace-glance.
contentBlocks.meanings: yathā rasa-sparśanataḥ = just as by contact with rasa (philosopher's stone/mercury) | ayasaḥ api = even iron | suvarṇatā syāt = gold-ness occurs | kamalē = O Kamalā (Lakṣmī) | tathā tē = similarly yours | kaṭākṣa-saṃsparśanataḥ = by contact with/touch of your grace-glance | janānām = of people | amaṅgalānām api = even of the inauspicious | maṅgalatvam = auspiciousness/blessing-nature

contentBlocks.original:
देहीति नास्तीति वचः प्रवेशा-
-द्भीतो रमे त्वां शरणं प्रपद्ये ।
अतः सदाऽस्मिन्नभयप्रदा त्वं
सहैव पत्या मयि सन्निधेहि ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dēhīti nāstīti vachaḥ pravēśā-
-dbhītō ramē tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē ।
ataḥ sadā'sminnabhayapradā tvaṃ
sahaiva patyā mayi sannidhēhi ॥ 91 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 91:] Afraid of [hearing] the word "there is nothing [I can give]" — I, frightened of "no," take refuge in you, O Ramē. Therefore always be close to me with your Lord, as the granter of fearlessness here.
contentBlocks.meanings: dēhīti = "give!" | nāsti iti vachaḥ = the word "there is nothing" | pravēśāt = from that entering [into the exchange] | bhīto = frightened | ramē = O Ramā (Lakṣmī) | tvāṃ śaraṇaṃ prapadyē = I take refuge in you | ataḥ sadā = therefore always | asmin = in this [devotee] | abhaya-pradā = granter of fearlessness | tvaṃ = you | saha ēva patyā = together with your Lord | mayi = in me | sannidhēhi = be near/present

contentBlocks.original:
कल्पद्रुमेण मणिना सहिता सुरम्या
श्रीस्ते कला मयि रसेन रसायनेन ।
आस्तां यतो मम शिरःकरदृष्टिपाद-
-स्पृष्टाः सुवर्णवपुषः स्थिरजङ्गमाः स्युः ॥ 92 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kalpadrumēṇa maṇinā sahitā suramyā
śrīstē kalā mayi rasēna rasāyanēna ।
āstāṃ yatō mama śiraḥkaradṛṣṭipāda-
-spṛṣṭāḥ suvarṇavapuṣaḥ sthirajaṅgamāḥ syuḥ ॥ 92 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 92:] May your lovely divine portion, together with the wish-tree and the wish-gem, act as an elixir (rasāyana) in me — so that whatever my head, hands, eyes, and feet touch may become golden in form, whether moving or stationary.
contentBlocks.meanings: kalpa-drumēṇa maṇinā sahitā = together with the wish-tree (kalpavṛkṣa) and the wish-gem | suramyā śrīḥ tē kalā = your beautiful divine portion | mayi rasēna rasāyanēna = as an elixir (rasāyana) | āstāṃ = may it abide | yataḥ = so that | mama śiraḥ-kara-dṛṣṭi-pāda-spṛṣṭāḥ = those touched by my head, hands, eyes, and feet | suvarṇa-vapuṣaḥ = of golden form | sthira-jaṅgamāḥ = moving and stationary things | syuḥ = may they become

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिविष्णोः स्थिरधर्मपत्नी
त्वमेव पत्या मयि सन्निधेहि ।
आद्यादिलक्ष्मि त्वदनुग्रहेण
पदे पदे मे निधिदर्शनं स्यात् ॥ 93 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādiviṣṇōḥ sthiradharmapatnī
tvamēva patyā mayi sannidhēhi ।
ādyādilakṣmi tvadanugrahēṇa
padē padē mē nidhidarśanaṃ syāt ॥ 93 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Stotram verse 93:] O steadfast righteous wife of Ādi-Viṣṇu — together with your Lord, come to me. O Ādi-Lakṣmī — by your grace, may treasure appear at my every step.
contentBlocks.meanings: ādyādi-viṣṇōḥ sthira-dharma-patnī = O steadfast righteous wife of Ādi-Viṣṇu | tvam ēva patyā mayi sannidhēhi = you alone, with your Lord, be near me | ādyādi-lakṣmi = O Ādi-Lakṣmī | tvad-anugrahēṇa = by your grace | padē padē = at every step | mē nidhi-darśanaṃ syāt = may there be discovery of treasure for me

contentBlocks.original:
आद्यादिलक्ष्मीहृदयं पठेद्यः
स राज्यलक्ष्मीमचलां तनोति ।
महादरिद्रोऽपि भवेद्धनाढ्य-
-स्तदन्वये श्रीः स्थिरतां प्रयाति ॥ 94 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ādyādilakṣmīhṛdayaṃ paṭhēdyaḥ
sa rājyalakṣmīmachalāṃ tanōti ।
mahādaridrō'pi bhavēddhanāḍhya-
-stadanvayē śrīḥ sthiratāṃ prayāti ॥ 94 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 94:] Whoever reads this Ādi-Lakṣmī Hṛdaya will attain steady royal fortune (rājya-lakṣmī). Even the very poor will become wealthy, and in their lineage Śrī will become permanently established.
contentBlocks.meanings: ādyādi-lakṣmī-hṛdayaṃ paṭhēt yaḥ = whoever reads this Ādi-Lakṣmī-Hṛdaya | sa rājya-lakṣmīm = he [attains] rājya-lakṣmī (royal/kingly fortune) | achalāṃ tanōti = spreads/establishes as immovable | mahā-daridraḥ api = even the very destitute | bhavēt dhanāḍhyaḥ = will become wealthy | tad-anvayē = in their lineage | śrīḥ sthiratāṃ prayāti = Śrī attains permanence

contentBlocks.original:
यस्य स्मरणमात्रेण तुष्टा स्याद्विष्णुवल्लभा ।
तस्याभीष्टं ददत्याशु तं पालयति पुत्रवत् ॥ 95 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
yasya smaraṇamātrēṇa tuṣṭā syādviṣṇuvallabhā ।
tasyābhīṣṭaṃ dadatyāśu taṃ pālayati putravat ॥ 95 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 95:] By whose mere remembrance the beloved of Viṣṇu (Lakṣmī) becomes pleased and quickly grants the devotee's desires, protecting them as a mother protects a son.
contentBlocks.meanings: yasya smaraṇa-mātrēṇa = by whose mere remembrance | tuṣṭā syāt = [Lakṣmī] becomes pleased | viṣṇu-vallabhā = the beloved of Viṣṇu | tasya abhīṣṭaṃ = that one's desires | dadati āśu = grants quickly | taṃ pālayati = protects him | putra-vat = like a son

contentBlocks.original:
इदं रहस्यं हृदयं सर्वकामफलप्रदम् ।
जपः पञ्चसहस्रं तु पुरश्चरणमुच्यते ॥ 96 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
idaṃ rahasyaṃ hṛdayaṃ sarvakāmaphalapradam ।
japaḥ pañchasahasraṃ tu puraścharaṇamuchyatē ॥ 96 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 96:] This Hṛdaya (Heart stotram) is a secret, the granter of all desired fruits. A recitation of five thousand times constitutes the puraścharaṇa (pre-initiation rite).
contentBlocks.meanings: idaṃ rahasyaṃ = this is a secret | hṛdayaṃ = the Hṛdaya stotram | sarva-kāma-phala-pradam = granter of all desired fruits | japaḥ pañcha-sahasraṃ = recitation of five thousand [times] | tu = indeed | puraścharaṇam uchyatē = is called the puraścharaṇa (preparatory rite)

contentBlocks.original:
त्रिकालमेककालं वा नरो भक्तिसमन्वितः ।
यः पठेच्छृणुयाद्वापि स याति परमां श्रियम् ॥ 97 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
trikālamēkakālaṃ vā narō bhaktisamanvitaḥ ।
yaḥ paṭhēchChṛṇuyādvāpi sa yāti paramāṃ śriyam ॥ 97 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 97:] Whoever reads this with devotion three times daily, or even once daily — will attain the supreme Śrī (the highest prosperity and grace).
contentBlocks.meanings: tri-kālam = three times [daily] (morning, noon, evening) | ēka-kālam vā = or once [daily] | naraḥ = a person | bhakti-samanvitaḥ = with devotion | yaḥ paṭhēt = whoever reads | śṛṇuyāt vā api = or even hears | sa yāti paramāṃ śriyam = he attains the supreme Śrī

contentBlocks.original:
महालक्ष्मीं समुद्दिश्य निशि भार्गववासरे ।
इदं श्रीहृदयं जप्त्वा पञ्चवारं धनी भवेत् ॥ 98 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
mahālakṣmīṃ samuddiśya niśi bhārgavavāsarē ।
idaṃ śrīhṛdayaṃ japtvā pañchavāraṃ dhanī bhavēt ॥ 98 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 98:] Reciting this Śrī-Hṛdaya five times, addressing Mahālakṣmī, on a Friday night — one will become wealthy.
contentBlocks.meanings: mahālakṣmīṃ samuddiśya = addressing Mahālakṣmī | niśi = at night | bhārgava-vāsarē = on Friday (the day of Bhārgava/Śukra/Venus, sacred to Lakṣmī) | idaṃ śrī-hṛdayaṃ japtvā = having recited this Śrī-Hṛdaya | pañcha-vāraṃ = five times | dhanī bhavēt = will become wealthy

contentBlocks.original:
अनेन हृदयेनान्नं गर्भिण्या अभिमन्त्रितम् ।
ददाति तत्कुले पुत्रो जायते श्रीपतिः स्वयम् ॥ 99 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
anēna hṛdayēnānnaṃ garbhiṇyā abhimantritam ।
dadāti tatkulē putrō jāyatē śrīpatiḥ svayam ॥ 99 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 99:] Food consecrated by this Hṛdaya and given to a pregnant woman — in her lineage a son will be born who is Viṣṇu himself.
contentBlocks.meanings: anēna hṛdayēna = with/by this Hṛdaya | annaṃ = food | garbhiṇyā = to a pregnant woman | abhimantritam = consecrated/charged [with this mantra] | dadāti = [if] given | tat-kulē = in that family/lineage | putraḥ jāyatē = a son is born | śrī-patiḥ svayam = Viṣṇu himself

contentBlocks.original:
नरेण वाऽथवा नार्या लक्ष्मीहृदयमन्त्रिते ।
जले पीते च तद्वंशे मन्दभाग्यो न जायते ॥ 100 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
narēṇa vā'thavā nāryā lakṣmīhṛdayamantritē ।
jalē pītē cha tadvaṃśē mandabhāgyō na jāyatē ॥ 100 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 100:] In the lineage of a man or woman who drinks water that has been consecrated by this Lakṣmī Hṛdaya mantra — no one of little fortune (mandabhāgya) will ever be born.
contentBlocks.meanings: narēṇa vā athavā nāryā = by a man or a woman | lakṣmī-hṛdaya-mantritē = [in] water consecrated by the Lakṣmī Hṛdaya | jalē pītē cha = and [if] that water is drunk | tat-vaṃśē = in that lineage | manda-bhāgyaḥ = one of little fortune | na jāyatē = will not be born

contentBlocks.original:
य आश्विने मासि च शुक्लपक्षे
रमोत्सवे सन्निहिते सुभक्त्या ।
पठेत्तथैकोत्तरवारवृद्ध्या
लभेत्स सौवर्णमयीं सुवृष्टिम् ॥ 101 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ya āśvinē māsi cha śuklapakṣē
ramōtsavē sannihitē subhaktyā ।
paṭhēttathaikōttaravāravṛddhyā
labhētsa sauvarṇamayīṃ suvṛṣṭim ॥ 101 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 101:] Whoever reads this stotram in the month of Āśvina (October), in the bright fortnight, at the Ramā festival (Dīpāvali), with sincere devotion — reading with one additional recitation each [day] — will attain a golden rain.
contentBlocks.meanings: yaḥ āśvinē māsi = whoever, in the month of Āśvina | śukla-pakṣē = in the bright fortnight | ramā-utsavē = at the Ramā festival (Lakṣmī Pūjā/Dīpāvali) | sannihitē = when present/near | subhaktyā = with great devotion | paṭhēt = reads | tathā ēka-uttara-vāra-vṛddhyā = with the addition of one more recitation [each day] | labhēt saḥ = will attain | sauvarṇamayīṃ su-vṛṣṭim = a golden rain

contentBlocks.original:
य एकभक्तोऽन्वहमेकवर्षं
विशुद्धधीः सप्ततिवारजापी ।
स मन्दभाग्योऽपि रमाकटाक्षा-
-द्भवेत्सहस्राक्षशताधिकश्रीः ॥ 102 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
ya ēkabhaktō'nvahamēkavarṣaṃ
viśuddhadhīḥ saptativārajāpī ।
sa mandabhāgyō'pi ramākaṭākṣā-
-dbhavētsahasrākṣaśatādhikaśrīḥ ॥ 102 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 102:] Whoever for a full year, eating once daily (ēkabhakta), with a purified mind, recites this seventy times daily — even if of little fortune, will attain by Ramā's grace-glance a prosperity exceeding a hundred times that of Indra.
contentBlocks.meanings: yaḥ = whoever | ēka-bhaktaḥ = eating once daily (ekabhakta vrata) | anvahan = every day | ēka-varṣaṃ = for one year | viśuddha-dhīḥ = with purified mind | saptati-vāra-jāpī = one who recites seventy times [daily] | sa manda-bhāgyaḥ api = even that one of little fortune | ramā-kaṭākṣāt = by Ramā's grace-glance | bhavēt = will become | sahasra-akṣa-śata-adhika-śrīḥ = of prosperity exceeding a hundred times that of Indra (the thousand-eyed one)

contentBlocks.original:
श्रीशाङ्घ्रिभक्तिं हरिदासदास्यं
प्रसन्नमन्त्रार्थदृढैकनिष्ठाम् ।
गुरोः स्मृतिं निर्मलबोधबुद्धिं
प्रदेहि मातः परमं पदं श्रीः ॥ 103 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
śrīśāṅghribhaktiṃ haridāsadāsyaṃ
prasannamantrārthadṛḍhaikaniṣṭhām ।
gurōḥ smṛtiṃ nirmalabōdhabuddhiṃ
pradēhi mātaḥ paramaṃ padaṃ śrīḥ ॥ 103 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 103:] O Mother, O Śrī — grant me devotion to Śrī Viṣṇu's feet, servitude to his devotees' servants, firm single-minded dedication to the mantra's meaning, constant remembrance of the guru, the wisdom of a pure, clear intellect, and the supreme state (mōkṣa).
contentBlocks.meanings: śrī-śāṅghri-bhaktiṃ = devotion to Śrī Viṣṇu's feet | hari-dāsa-dāsyaṃ = servitude to Hari's devotees' servants | prasanna-mantra-artha-dṛḍha-ēka-niṣṭhām = firm, single-minded dedication to the clear meaning of the mantra | guroḥ smṛtiṃ = remembrance of the guru | nirmala-bōdha-buddhiṃ = wisdom of a pure, clear intelligence | pradēhi = grant | mātar = O Mother | paramaṃ padaṃ śrīḥ = the supreme state, O Śrī

contentBlocks.original:
पृथ्वीपतित्वं पुरुषोत्तमत्वं
विभूतिवासं विविधार्थसिद्धिम् ।
सम्पूर्णकीर्तिं बहुवर्षभोगं
प्रदेहि मे लक्ष्मि पुनः पुनस्त्वम् ॥ 104 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
pṛthvīpatitvaṃ puruṣōttamatvaṃ
vibhūtivāsaṃ vividhārthasiddhim ।
sampūrṇakīrtiṃ bahuvarṣabhōgaṃ
pradēhi mē lakṣmi punaḥ punastvam ॥ 104 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 104:] O Lakṣmī — again and again, grant me: sovereignty over the earth, supreme excellence, dwelling in glory, attainment of various purposes, complete fame, and many years of enjoyment.
contentBlocks.meanings: pṛthvī-patitvaṃ = sovereignty over the earth | puruṣōttama-tvaṃ = supreme excellence (the quality of being the best among men) | vibhūti-vāsaṃ = dwelling in glory/prosperity | vividha-artha-siddhim = attainment of various purposes | sampūrṇa-kīrtiṃ = complete fame | bahu-varṣa-bhōgaṃ = enjoyment of many years | pradēhi mē lakṣmi = grant me, O Lakṣmī | punaḥ punaḥ tvam = you, again and again

contentBlocks.original:
वादार्थसिद्धिं बहुलोकवश्यं
वयः स्थिरत्वं ललनासुभोगम् ।
पौत्रादिलब्धिं सकलार्थसिद्धिं
प्रदेहि मे भार्गवि जन्मजन्मनि ॥ 105 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
vādārthasiddhiṃ bahulōkavaśyaṃ
vayaḥ sthiratvaṃ lalanāsubhōgam ।
pautrādilabdhiṃ sakalārthasiddhiṃ
pradēhi mē bhārgavi janmajanmani ॥ 105 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 105:] O Bhārgavī — birth after birth, grant me: victory in debate, attracting many people, stable youthfulness, enjoyment of noble women, attainment of grandchildren and other descendants, and the fulfilment of all purposes.
contentBlocks.meanings: vāda-artha-siddhiṃ = victory in the purpose of debate | bahulōka-vaśyaṃ = bringing many people under favourable influence | vayaḥ sthiratvaṃ = stable/firm youthfulness | lalanā-su-bhōgam = enjoyment of noble/beautiful women | pautra-ādi-labdhiṃ = attainment of grandchildren and other [descendants] | sakala-artha-siddhiṃ = attainment of all purposes | pradēhi mē = grant me | bhārgavi = O Bhārgavī | janma-janmani = birth after birth

contentBlocks.original:
सुवर्णवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः
सुधान्यवृद्धिं कुरू मे गृहे श्रीः ।
कल्याणवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः
विभूतिवृद्धिं कुरु मे गृहे श्रीः ॥ 106 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
suvarṇavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ
sudhānyavṛddhiṃ kurū mē gṛhē śrīḥ ।
kalyāṇavṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ
vibhūtivṛddhiṃ kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ ॥ 106 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Phalaśruti verse 106:] O Śrī — increase gold in my home! O Śrī — increase auspicious grain in my home! O Śrī — increase welfare and auspiciousness in my home! O Śrī — increase splendour and glory in my home!
contentBlocks.meanings: suvarṇa-vṛddhiṃ = increase in gold | kuru mē gṛhē śrīḥ = O Śrī, bring about in my home | su-dhānya-vṛddhiṃ = increase in good grain | kalyāṇa-vṛddhiṃ = increase in welfare/auspiciousness | vibhūti-vṛddhiṃ = increase in splendour/glory

contentBlocks.original:
ध्यायेल्लक्ष्मीं प्रहसितमुखीं कोटिबालार्कभासां
विद्युद्वर्णाम्बरवरधरां भूषणाढ्यां सुशोभाम् ।
बीजापूरं सरसिजयुगं बिभ्रतीं स्वर्णपात्रं
भर्त्रायुक्तां मुहुरभयदां मह्यमप्यच्युतश्रीः ॥ 107 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dhyāyēllakṣmīṃ prahasitamukhīṃ kōṭibālārkabhāsāṃ
vidyudvarṇāmbaravaradharāṃ bhūṣaṇāḍhyāṃ suśōbhām ।
bījāpūraṃ sarasijayugaṃ bibhratīṃ svarṇapātraṃ
bhartrāyuktāṃ muhurabhayadāṃ mahyamapyachyutaśrīḥ ॥ 107 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Final Dhyāna verse (verse 107):] One should meditate on Lakṣmī: with a gently smiling face, radiant as a crore of morning suns, wearing lightning-coloured garments, holding lotuses and the boon-granting gesture, adorned with every ornament, supremely beautiful — holding the citron (bījāpūra) fruit and a pair of lotuses in one hand, and a golden pot in another, filling all the space of the three worlds with her radiance.
contentBlocks.meanings: dhyāyēt lakṣmīṃ = meditate on Lakṣmī | prahasita-mukhīṃ = with a smiling/laughing face | kōṭi-bāla-arka-bhāsāṃ = with the brilliance of a crore of morning suns | vidyud-varṇa-ambarā = wearing lightning-coloured garments | vara-dharāṃ = holding the boon-granting [gesture] | bhūṣaṇa-āḍhyāṃ = adorned with ornaments | su-śōbhām = supremely beautiful | bījāpūraṃ = citron fruit | sarasa-ija-yugaṃ = a pair of water-lilies | bibhratīṃ = holding | svarṇa-pātraṃ = golden vessel | bhara-nakha-rasā = filling [space] with the juice/essence of her nails

contentBlocks.original:
गुह्यातिगुह्यगोप्त्री त्वं गृहाणास्मत्कृतं जपम् ।
सिद्धिर्भवतु मे देवि त्वत्प्रसादान्मयि स्थिता ॥ 108 ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
guhyātiguhyagōptrī tvaṃ gṛhāṇāsmatkṛtaṃ japam ।
siddhirbhavatu mē dēvi tvatprasādānmayi sthitā ॥ 108 ॥
contentBlocks.translation: [Final verse (verse 108):] O you who are the guardian of the most secret of all secrets — accept our japa (recitation). O Goddess, may the siddhi (perfection/fulfilment) that rests in me come about by your grace.
contentBlocks.meanings: guhya-ati-guhya-gōptrī tvaṃ = O you who are the guardian of the most secret of all secrets | gṛhāṇa asmatkṛtaṃ japam = accept the japa done by us | siddhiḥ bhavatu mē dēvi = may siddhi come to me, O Goddess | tvat-prasādān = by your grace | mayi sthitā = resting in me

contentBlocks.original: इति श्रीअथर्वणरहस्ये श्रीलक्ष्मीहृदयस्तोत्रं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration: iti śrīatharvaṇarahasyē śrīlakṣmīhṛdayastōtraṃ sampūrṇam ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Thus concludes the Śrī Lakṣmī Hṛdaya Stotram from the Atharvaṇa Rahasya.
